## lattice-system

A Lean 4 + Mathlib formalization project targeting general lattice models.
This project subsumes and generalizes the earlier
[ising-model](https://github.com/phasetr/ising-model) project, progressively
covering classical spin systems, quantum spin systems, Hubbard, BCS,
CAR algebras, and eventually lattice QCD.

## Design axis: graphs, not lattices

Despite the name, the **primary combinatorial abstraction in this
library is a graph `(╬ؤ, E_╬ؤ)`** ظ¤ finite for finite-volume work and
infinite for the thermodynamic-limit / algebraic-formulation work
that is a major long-term goal ظ¤ not "a lattice". Concrete lattices
(the 1D chain, square / cubic grids, infinite chains, ظج^d, ظخ) appear
only as specific instances such as `SimpleGraph.pathGraph`,
`SimpleGraph.cycleGraph`, products of these, or their infinite
analogues. This convention follows the standard mathematical-physics
literature on many-body systems on graphs (Lieb's theorem on
bipartite lattices, the MarshallظôLiebظôMattis theorem, Miyao 2021
┬د3, ظخ) and aligns the project with mathlib's `SimpleGraph`
foundations.

Finite-volume work uses `╬ؤ : Type*` together with `[Fintype ╬ؤ]`
when needed (e.g. for traces, partition functions, finite sums of
local terms); infinite-volume work drops the `Fintype` assumption
and uses graphs over types like `ظج` or `ظج^d` instead.

The bridge from a `SimpleGraph` to the pairwise coupling
`J : ╬ؤ ظْ ╬ؤ ظْ ظé` consumed by `heisenbergHamiltonian` (and similar
operators) lives in `LatticeSystem.Lattice.Graph` (`couplingOf`).
Existing chain coupling families (`openChainCoupling`,
`periodicChainCoupling`) are characterised as
`couplingOf (pathGraph _) _` and `couplingOf (cycleGraph _) _`
respectively.

## Scope

| Area | Stage | Typical references |
|---|---|---|
| Classical spin systems | Inherited from ising-model | Friedli-Velenik, Glimm-Jaffe |
| Quantum spin systems | Current focus | Tasaki, Nielsen-Chuang (cross-check) |
| Hubbard / BCS | Medium term | Tasaki 1998, Bru-Pedra |
| CAR-algebraic formulation | Medium-long term | Araki-Moriya, Bru-Pedra |
| Thermodynamic limit (infinite graphs) | Long term, **major project goal** | Simon, Friedli-Velenik, Bratteli-Robinson |
| Lattice QCD | Longest term | Aarts, Davies |

## Refactoring conventions and review criteria

A **single-source-of-truth document** for refactoring conventions
applied as the review checklist on every pull request:
[Refactoring conventions and review criteria](refactoring-conventions.html).
Topics: test methods (decide / bridge / small-exhaustive / shim /
`#guard_msgs`), module-split criteria, generic / dedup conventions,
deprecation window policy, naming / docstring rules, linter
exceptions, public-doc synchronisation.

The companion page [Deprecation window](deprecations.html) tracks
currently-deprecated public declarations, the `since` date,
recommended replacement, and earliest-removal window.

hermitianMinEigenvalue` hypothesis explicit). **Target balanced-sector PF discharge** (Issue #3739): off-diagonal anisotropic/Heisenberg agreement, real sector matrices, the Marshall-dressed shifted anisotropic sector matrix, structural irreducibility, and CollatzظôWielandt PF/minimum identification now provide the balanced-sector `finrank ظé ظëج 1` input at the spin-1/2 target point and in the general spin-S target sector; the PF-free target wrappers no longer take an explicit `h_balanced_sector_pf` callback. **Strict-gap, SU(2)-uniqueness, and MLM endpoint wrappers** now replace the explicit balanced/full equality input by the standard strict gap over all non-balanced non-empty magnetization sectors, derive that strict gap from SU(2)-endpoint global uniqueness, and then construct the SU(2) uniqueness input from the general Theorem 2.3 MLM/Casimir/PF endpoint. **D-boundary extension**: the spin-1/2 parity-block route has a dedicated `D ظëح 0` version because single-ion `┬▒2` moves are impossible for `Fin (1+1)`. The general spin-S route now also has a bond-only parity reachability replacement for the single-ion branch, giving general `D ظëح 0` parity-block irreducibility and the MLM/Casimir target wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general`. **╬╗=1 boundary**: at spin `1/2`, `(S^3)^2 = (1/4)I`, so `singleIonAnisotropyS D 1` is a scalar shift and `anisotropicHeisenbergS J 1 D 1` reduces to the Heisenberg Hamiltonian plus `D|╬ؤ|/4`; the SU(2) Theorem 2.3/MLM uniqueness transfers to target uniqueness and zero magnetization at `╬╗ = 1`. For general spin-S, the SU(2)-point wrappers cover `╬╗ = 1`, `D = 0`, and for `2 ظëج N` the ion-only parity reachability route gives the `╬╗ = 1`, `D > 0` endpoint wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general`. **Case (ii) bridges**: the case-(ii) path-region lemmas show the deformation path stays in `╬╗ ظëح 1`, `D ظëج 0`; the first case-(ii) target wrappers reuse the balanced-sector PF bridge while leaving balanced-sector/full-ground equality plus full `finrank <= 2` explicit; the strict-gap wrappers replace the direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input by the strict sector gap; the no-full-finrank wrappers then use sector projection and Hermitian below-min exclusion to remove the full `finrank <= 2` input once strict gap is supplied; the crossing-callback bridge compresses the remaining strict-gap derivation to a single target crossing contradiction callback; the path-callback bridge reduces that target callback to a contradiction for crossings along the case-(ii) path; the crossing-set bridge reduces the path callback to contradiction for non-empty `perMCrossingSet M ظêر Icc 0 1`; the first-crossing bridge reduces crossing-set non-emptiness to contradiction at `sInf (perMCrossingSet M ظêر Icc 0 1)`; the argmin-first-crossing bridge reduces the target contradiction to the sector whose first crossing minimises those `sInf` values; the first-crossing finrank bridge proves that argmin callback from a `finrank <= 2` bound at the selected first crossing; the path-global finrank bridge moves this to every `t ظêê Icc 0 1`; the block-path finrank bridge reduces it to full-ground-energy parity-block submatrix simplicity; and the block-PF/min bridge reduces that to pathwise bare parity-block PF simplicity plus PF/min identification; and the parity-gauge sign layer introduces the case-(ii) block gauge needed to flip exactly the ┬▒2 parity-bond and single-ion moves; and the local sign layer proves strict shifted-entry positivity for transverse, parity-bond, and single-ion elementary moves; and the block reachability, block non-negativity, step-support, raw support-classification, and boundary move-set bridges reduce parity-block irreducibility to conditional irreducibility under the bipartite support-zero assumption on `J`. | The public general spin-S `2 ظëج N` parameter-region wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` package the general spin-S surface. The spin-`1/2` wrappers `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` and `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` now package the exact `N = 1` surface: `-1 < ╬╗ < 1`, `D ظëح 0`; `╬╗ = 1`, using the scalar-shift boundary; and strict case (ii) `1 < ╬╗`, `D ظëج 0`. |

with the lower bound #4326 ظا╣ `finrank G = N+2`; axiom-free); **PR3-cap `TJHalfFillingMaximalSpin.lean` `tJ_halfFilling_isMaximalSpinMultiplet`** (the half-filling t-J ground subspace is the maximal-spin `(N+2)`-fold multiplet: `IsMaximalSpinMultipletSubmodule N G (N+1)`, assembling `finrank=N+2` with the all-up SU(2) tower spanning `G`; boundary case of Theorem 11.26's t-J side; axiom-free, no A.17); **PR-unify `TJMaximalSpinUnified.lean` `tJ_isMaximalSpinMultiplet_of_le`** (the d=1 t-J ground subspace is the maximal-spin multiplet for ALL odd `Ne ظëج K+1`: metallic `Ne<K+1` via `proposition_11_24` + half-filling `Ne=K+1`; the half-filling chain generalized to drop `0<N` so `K=0` is covered; t-J input to Theorem 11.26, rests on A.17 via Prop 11.24); **PR-final: `theorem_11_26` is now a PROVED theorem** (`MetallicFerroModel.lean`, Issue #4314): discharged from `lemma_11_25` (documented strong-coupling-equivalence axiom) + `tJ_isMaximalSpinMultiplet_of_le`; `#print axioms theorem_11_26` = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound, lemma_11_25]` (A.17's `exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate` now discharged ┬دA.3.2). pairing with `╧ê ظêê ker V╠é` removes the `vV╠é` term outright and the limit is the weak equation). **Appendix A.2 complete.** **┬دA.3 angular momentum**: **Lemma A.14** (su(2) ladder) ظ¤ operator identity `─┤ظ╗─┤ظ║=─┤┬▓ظêْ─┤┬│(─┤┬│+1)` (eq A.3.7) + ladder non-vanishing (`ظêْJظëجM<J`, `╬خظëب0` ظçْ `─┤ظ║╬خظëب0`) + raising **membership** (`─┤ظ║╬خظêêH_{J,M+1}`, via `[─┤┬│,─┤ظ║]=─┤ظ║` + `[─┤┬▓,─┤ظ║]=0`) + **lowering** direction (`─┤ظ║─┤ظ╗=─┤┬▓ظêْ─┤┬│(─┤┬│ظêْ1)` eq A.3.8, `─┤ظ╗╬خظëب0` for `ظêْJ<MظëجJ`, `─┤ظ╗╬خظêêH_{J,Mظêْ1}`) **proved** ظ¤ A.14 fully done (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Ladder.lean`). **Lemma A.15 (spin bound, part 1)**: norm identities `ظû─┤┬▒╬خظû┬▓={J(J+1)ظêْM(M┬▒1)}ظû╬خظû┬▓` (eq A.3.9, Hermitian ─┤^╬▒) `angRaise/angLower_normSq` + `angMom_abs_le_J` (`ظêْJظëجMظëجJ`, i.e. `JظêْM,J+Mظëح0`) **proved** (axiom-free). **Lemma A.15 (integrality)** + **Theorem A.13 (`J = n/2`)**: ladder-termination `raiseIter` (iterated `─┤ظ║`) with `raiseIter_eigenspace`/`raiseIter_ne_zero` gives `JظêْM ظêê ظجظëح0` (`angMom_sub_mem_nat`); the gauge-reflected `(─┤┬╣,ظêْ─┤┬▓,ظêْ─┤┬│)` gives `J+M ظêê ظجظëح0`, so `2J=(JظêْM)+(J+M)ظêêظجظëح0`, i.e. `J=n/2` (`angMom_J_eq_half_nat`) **proved** (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Quantization.lean`). **Theorem A.16 (SU(2)-multiplet degeneracy)**: for SU(2)-invariant `─ج` (`[─ج,─┤ط╡à]=0`), a joint energy eigenstate in `H_{J,M0}` yields nonzero same-energy companions in every `H_{J,Jظêْk}` (`kظëج2J`) ظ¤ `ham_su2_multiplet` (energy `E` is ظëح`(2J+1)`-fold degenerate), proved via raise-to-top + reflected-lowering ladder + `ham_mulVec_raiseIter` (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Multiplet.lean`). **Theorem A.17 (spin-0/1-2 sector suffices)**: every energy eigenvalue of an SU(2)-invariant `─ج` has an eigenstate with `─┤┬│=0` or `─┤┬│=1/2` ظ¤ `ham_eigenstate_spin_zero_or_half` (corollary of A.16+A.13; the lone simultaneous-diagonalization step `exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate` is **now proved (axiom-free)** via the generic common eigenvector of two commuting Hermitian operators on an invariant subspace (`Math/CommutingHermitianEigenvector.lean`: `exists_common_eigenvector_of_isHermitian_commute`), the Casimir `─┤┬▓` being PSD and commuting with `─┤┬│`/`─ج`, `AngularMomentum/SpinHalfSector.lean`). **Theorem A.18 (PerronظôFrobenius, real symmetric)**: a real symmetric `M` with `(c┬╖1ظêْM)` irreducible (off-diag `ظëج0` + connectivity) has a nondegenerate lowest eigenvalue with a strictly positive eigenvector ظ¤ `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric` (reuses the project's CollatzظôWielandt PF + eigenspace simplicity; the lowest-eigenvalue identification is the variational `|w|`-argument eq A.4.1), proved (axiom-free, `PerronFrobeniusSymmetric.lean`). **Theorems A.19/A.20 (polar + SVD)**: `matrix_polar_decomposition` (`A = W C`, `W` unitary, `C` PSD) and `matrix_singular_value_decomposition` (`A = U D Vظب`, `U,V` unitary, `D = diagonal d` with `d ظëح 0`) ظ¤ **now proved (axiom-free)**: SVD built from the spectral theorem of `Aط┤┤A` (`eigenvectorUnitary` `V`, eigenvalues `╬╗_i ظëح 0`, `d_i = ظêأ╬╗_i`), with the normalised images `u_i = d_iظ╗┬╣┬╖A v_i` extended to an orthonormal basis (`exists_orthonormalBasis_extension_of_card_eq`) giving `U`, and `A V = U D` columnwise; polar follows as `W = U Vط┤┤`, `C = V D Vط┤┤` (`MatrixAnalysis/Decomposition.lean`). **Theorems A.21/A.22 (Wigner)**: `wignerAutomorphism_unitary` (linear ظêù-automorphism of `M(H)` ظا╣ `╬ô(├é)=├ؤظب├é├ؤ`) + `wignerAutomorphism_antiunitary` (antilinear ظا╣ `├ؤظب├é╠├ؤ`) + `wignerProjection` (rank-1-projection map preserving `Tr[P Pظ▓]` ظا╣ (anti)unitary `V╠é`) ظ¤ documented axioms over `Matrix D D ظé` (`WignerTheorem.lean`, Issue #4224). **Definition A.23 + Theorem A.24 (states / BanachظôAlaoglu)**: `IsState` (weak-ظêù-continuous `╧ : WeakDual ظé A` with `╧ 1 = 1`, `0 ظëج ╧(aظبa)`) over an abstract unital C*-algebra + `stateSpace_isCompact` (state space weak-ظêù compact, documented axiom) ظ¤ `CStarAlgebra/State.lean`. **Definitions A.25/A.27 + Theorem A.26 (ground states)**: dynamics modelled by a derivation `╬┤=[─ج,┬╖]`; `IsGroundState` (`0 ظëج ╧ë(aظب┬╖╬┤a)`) + `HasNonzeroGap` (`╬│>0`, `╧ë(aظب╬┤a) ظëح ╬│╧ë(aظبa)` on `╧ë(a)=0`) + `groundState_variational` (ground state ظا║ `╧ë(─ج_L)` least over states agreeing outside ╬ؤ_L, documented axiom) ظ¤ `CStarAlgebra/GroundState.lean`. **Theorem A.28 (GNS construction)**: `gns_construction` ظ¤ every state `╧` on a C*-algebra has a GNS triple `(H_╧, ╧_╧, ╬ر_╧)` (`ظêù`-representation `╧ : A ظْظïظé[ظé] (HظْL H)` + cyclic `╬ر`) with `╧(├é)=ظاذ╬ر,╧(├é)╬رظار` and `{╧(├é)╬ر}` dense; documented axiom (mathlib has the GNS machinery), `CStarAlgebra/GNS.lean`. **┬دA.6ظô┬دA.7 (Wigner / states / ground states / GNS) complete ظْ Appendix A complete.** Prove-first where mathlib supports, axiomatize-first for the heavy analytic results, strict book order (see the status & axiomatization-policy note below). |

### Appendix A: status and axiomatization policy

Tasaki's **Appendix A is fully formalized in book order (A.1ظôA.28)**, and the
entire Tasaki text up to and including Chapter 11 (┬د11.5) plus this appendix is
now covered. The appendix splits into two kinds of items:

- **Proved (axiom-free)** ظ¤ the linear-algebra and angular-momentum core that
  `mathlib` supports directly: **A.1** (the Lie product / Trotter formula, proved
  from scratch in a complete normed `ظإ`-algebra), **A.12** (the strong-coupling
  effective Hamiltonian limit, by the `vظ╗┬╣`-squeeze + kernel-pairing continuity
  argument), **A.4ظôA.6**
  (positive-semidefinite basics + unique
  PSD square root), **A.7ظôA.8** (Weyl eigenvalue monotonicity via CourantظôFischer,
  and the trace-exp monotonicity through the spectral mapping for `exp`),
  **A.9ظôA.11** (frustration-free Hamiltonians, energy-form
  kernels), **A.13ظôA.16** (angular-momentum quantization `J = n/2`, the ladder,
  and SU(2)-multiplet degeneracy), **A.17** (spin-0/half sector via the common
  eigenvector of commuting Hermitians), **A.18** (PerronظôFrobenius for a real
  symmetric matrix, via the project's CollatzظôWielandt development and the
  variational `|w|`-argument), and **A.19ظôA.20** (polar + singular-value
  decompositions from the spectral theorem).
- **Documented axioms (faithful statements, deferred proofs)** ظ¤ the
  operator-algebraic **A.21ظôA.28** (Wigner's
  theorem, states, BanachظôAlaoglu, ground states of infinite systems, the GNS
  construction).

**Value judgment / policy.** The documented axioms above are kept as *faithful,
book-order statements* ظ¤ they record exactly what Tasaki proves ظ¤ but they are
**not active proof targets** of this project. The heavy functional-analytic and
operator-algebraic results (states on the quasi-local C\*-algebra, weak-ظêù
compactness, ground states, GNS, Wigner) belong to a dedicated operator-algebra /
functional-analysis development; such a development may well be carried out
**separately** (for instance contributed to `mathlib`), and these axioms simply
**wait for that implementation**. Accordingly the project's policy is to
**axiomatize only the appendix results that Tasaki's formalized main development
actually uses**, to **prove** the remaining ones where `mathlib` provides the
tools, and otherwise to leave a faithful axiom in place rather than invest in a
large bespoke operator-algebra build whose natural home is elsewhere. The
`#print axioms` of every theorem in the repository makes the precise dependency
on these documented axioms auditable.

## Formalized theorems

All items below are formally proved with **zero `sorry`**. Full
mathematical statements and proof sketches are in
[`tex/proof-guide.tex`](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/blob/main/tex/proof-guide.tex).

### Single-site Pauli operators

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬د2.1 eq. (2.1.8), p. 15. Cross-checked with Nielsen-Chuang
┬د2.1.3 Figure 2.2 (pp. 65-66, definitions), Ex. 2.19 (p. 70,
Hermiticity), Ex. 2.41 (p. 78, `(╧â^╬▒)┬▓ = I` and anticommutation),
Ex. 2.40 (p. 77, commutator, whence the cyclic products).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `pauliX/Y/Z_isHermitian` | `(╧â^╬▒)ظب = ╧â^╬▒` | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX/Y/Z_mul_self` | `(╧â^╬▒)┬▓ = I` | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX_mul_pauliY` etc. | `╧â^x ╧â^y = i┬╖╧â^z` (cyclic) | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX_anticomm_pauliY` etc. | `╧â^╬▒ ╧â^╬▓ + ╧â^╬▓ ╧â^╬▒ = 0` (╬▒ ظëب ╬▓) | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |

| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_pi_conj_spinHalfOp_*` | axis invariance and sign flip at ╬╕=╧ (Tasaki (2.1.15)/(2.1.21)) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_spinHalfOp{2,3,1}` | `(├ؤ^(╬▒)_╬╕)ظب ┼£^(╬▓) ├ؤ^(╬▒)_╬╕ = cos ╬╕ ┬╖ ┼£^(╬▓) - sin ╬╕ ┬╖ ┼£^(╬│)` (Tasaki eq. (2.1.16), even-╬╡ cyclic triple) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_spinHalfOp{3,1,2}` | `(├ؤ^(╬▒)_╬╕)ظب ┼£^(╬▓) ├ؤ^(╬▒)_╬╕ = cos ╬╕ ┬╖ ┼£^(╬▓) + sin ╬╕ ┬╖ ┼£^(╬│)` (Tasaki eq. (2.1.16), odd-╬╡ triple) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_spinHalfOp{1,2,3}` | same-axis invariance `(├ؤ^(╬▒)_╬╕)ظب ┼£^(╬▒) ├ؤ^(╬▒)_╬╕ = ┼£^(╬▒)` (Tasaki eq. (2.1.15)) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot{1,2,3}_half_pi_conj_spinHalfOp_*` | `╧/2`-rotation conjugation `(├ؤ^(╬▒)_{╧/2})ظب ┼£^(╬▓) ├ؤ^(╬▒)_{╧/2} = -╬╡^{╬▒╬▓╬│} ┼£^(╬│)` (Tasaki eq. (2.1.22), 6 cases) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_eq_exp` | `├ؤ^(3)_╬╕ = exp(-i╬╕ ┼£^(3))` via `Matrix.exp_diagonal` + Euler (Problem 2.1.b, axis 3) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_mul_spinHalfRot2_mulVec_spinHalfUp` | `├ؤ^(3)_╧ ├ؤ^(2)_╬╕ |╧ê^ظّظار = e^{-i╧/2} cos(╬╕/2) |╧ê^ظّظار + e^{i╧/2} sin(╬╕/2) |╧ê^ظôظار` (coherent state, Problem 2.1.d) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_mul_spinHalfRot2_mulVec_spinHalfDown` | `├ؤ^(3)_╧ ├ؤ^(2)_╬╕ |╧ê^ظôظار = -e^{-i╧/2} sin(╬╕/2) |╧ê^ظّظار + e^{i╧/2} cos(╬╕/2) |╧ê^ظôظار` (rotation of spin-down, Problem 2.2.c auxiliary) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_half_pi_mul_spinHalfRot2_half_pi_mulVec_spinHalfUp` | specialization at ╬╕ = ╧ = ╧/2 (Problem 2.1.e) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfDotVec` / `spinHalfDotVec_isHermitian` | vector inner product `┼£ ┬╖ v := ╬ث_╬▒ v_╬▒ ┼£^(╬▒)` and its Hermiticity (cf. (2.1.19)) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_commute_spinHalfOp3_smul` | same-axis rotation commutes with `v ┬╖ ┼£^(3)` (cf. (2.1.20) along axis) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `hadamard` / `hadamard_mul_self` | the Hadamard basis-change matrix `W = (1/ظêأ2)┬╖!![1,1;1,-1]` and `W┬╖W = 1` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `hadamard_mul_spinHalfOp1_mul_hadamard` | `W ┬╖ ┼£^(1) ┬╖ W = ┼£^(3)` (basis change between ╧â^x and ╧â^z) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `hadamard_mul_spinHalfOp3_mul_hadamard` | `W ┬╖ ┼£^(3) ┬╖ W = ┼£^(1)` (inverse basis change) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1_eq_hadamard_conj` | `├ؤ^(1)_╬╕ = W ┬╖ ├ؤ^(3)_╬╕ ┬╖ W` (axis 1 rotation as Hadamard conjugate of axis 3) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1_eq_exp` | `├ؤ^(1)_╬╕ = exp(-i╬╕ ┼£^(1))` via Hadamard conjugation + `Matrix.exp_conj` (Problem 2.1.b, axis 1) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `yDiag` / `yDiagAdj` / `yDiag_mul_yDiagAdj` / `yDiag_mul_spinHalfOp3_mul_yDiagAdj` | y-axis basis-change unitary `V` with `V┬╖Vظب = 1` and `V┬╖┼£^(3)┬╖Vظب = ┼£^(2)` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot2_eq_yDiag_conj` / `spinHalfRot2_eq_exp` | `├ؤ^(2)_╬╕ = V┬╖├ؤ^(3)_╬╕┬╖Vظب` and `├ؤ^(2)_╬╕ = exp(-i╬╕ ┼£^(2))` (Problem 2.1.b, axis 2) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |

### Zظéé ├ù Zظéé representation (Tasaki ┬د2.1 eqs. (2.1.27)-(2.1.34))

The Zظéé ├ù Zظéé structure is proved across files:
- S = 1/2 (projective, eq. (2.1.31)): `spinHalfRot*_pi_sq = -1`, anticommutation, products.
- S = 1 (genuine, eq. (2.1.27)): `spinOnePiRot*_sq = 1`, commutation.

See `Quantum/Z2Z2.lean` for the unified documentation.

| `spinOneUnit{10,21}_eq_polynomial` | `(1/ظêأ2) ┼£^+ ┬╖ P_╧â` for the two single-step raising units | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |

### Spin-`S` operators (general S ظëح 0, parameterised by `N = 2S : ظـ`)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body Systems*, ┬د2.1 Problem 2.1.a (p. 15) and solution S.1 (p. 493).

Generic spin-`S` operators live on `Matrix (Fin (N + 1)) (Fin (N + 1)) ظé`, with `N = 2S : ظـ` (so `N = 1` ظç¤ `S = 1/2`, `N = 2` ظç¤ `S = 1`). Tracking issue #458 (Tasaki ┬د2.1 P1d''' general-S generalisation).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `spinSOp3` | `┼£^(3) := diag(N/2, N/2 - 1, ظخ, -N/2)` for `N : ظـ` | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus` / `spinSOpMinus` | raising/lowering operators with matrix entries `ظêأ(k┬╖(N ظêْ k + 1))` (resp. `ظêأ((N ظêْ k)(k + 1))`) on the immediate sub/super-diagonal | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp1` / `spinSOp2` | `┼£^(1) := (┼£^+ + ┼£^-) / 2`, `┼£^(2) := (┼£^+ ظêْ ┼£^-) / (2 i)` | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_apply_top` / `_bottom` | `┼£^+` annihilates `\|Nظار` (highest weight); `┼£^-` annihilates `\|0ظار` (lowest weight) ظ¤ the ladder boundaries | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_commutator_spinSOp{Plus,Minus}` | **Cartan relations** `[┼£^{(3)}, ┼£^+] = ┼£^+` and `[┼£^{(3)}, ┼£^-] = -┼£^-`: `┼£^┬▒` shift the magnetic quantum number by `┬▒1`. Proved entry-wise via `Matrix.diagonal_mul` / `mul_diagonal` (╬▓-2 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Algebra.lean` |
| `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_conjTranspose` / `spinSOp{1,2,3}_isHermitian` | adjointness `(┼£^+)ط┤┤ = ┼£^-`, `(┼£^-)ط┤┤ = ┼£^+`, and Hermiticity of `┼£^{(╬▒)}` for `╬▒ ظêê {1, 2, 3}` (╬▓-3 of Issue #458). The ladder adjointness follows from the matrix-entry symmetry; `┼£^{(1)}` and `┼£^{(2)}` use the `1/2` and `1/(2i)` self-conjugacy facts | `Quantum/SpinS/Hermitian.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj` / `spinSOp3_sub_smul_mul_diagProj` / `_self_mul_diagProj` | the **diagonal projector** `P_k := diag(╬┤_{i,k})` and the eigenvalue-action lemma `(┼£^{(3)} ظêْ ╬╗_j ظت 1) ┬╖ P_k = (╬╗_k ظêْ ╬╗_j) ┬╖ P_k`, with the annihilation specialisation at `j = k` (╬▓-4 of Issue #458). Foundation for the Lagrange-interpolation polynomial decomposition: each `P_k` will be expressed as `ظê_{j ظëب k} (┼£^{(3)} ظêْ ╬╗_j ظت 1) / (╬╗_k ظêْ ╬╗_j)` in a follow-up PR | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProj.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_mul_diagProj` / `diagProj_mul_spinSOp3` / `spinSOp3_commute_diagProj` | the **eigenvalue equation** `┼£^{(3)} ┬╖ P_k = ╬╗_k ┬╖ P_k` (where `╬╗_k = (N : ظé)/2 ظêْ k`), with the symmetric `P_k ┬╖ ┼£^{(3)} = ╬╗_k ┬╖ P_k` and the resulting commutativity. Both are diagonal-times-diagonal computations (╬▓-5 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Lagrange.lean` |
| `mul_diagProj_apply` / `diagProj_mul_apply` / `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_mul_diagProj_apply` | **Column/row selection** by the diagonal projector: `(A ┬╖ P_k)[i, j] = A[i, k]` if `j = k` else `0` (and dually for `P_k ┬╖ A`). Specialised to `┼£^┬▒` produces off-diagonal matrix units (╬▓-6 of Issue #458) ظ¤ the building blocks of the polynomial decomposition theorem | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderProj.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_succ_mul_spinSOpMinus` | **Ladder recursion**: `┼£^+ ┬╖ P_{k+1} ┬╖ ┼£^- = (k + 1)(N ظêْ k) ┬╖ P_k`. The triple product collapses to a non-zero scalar multiple of `P_k`. Iterating from the lowest-weight projector `P_N` (itself a polynomial in `┼£^{(3)}`) yields every diagonal projector as a polynomial in `{1╠é, ┼£^{(╬▒)}}` (╬▓-7 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderRecursion.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_first` / `spinSOpMinus_mul_diagProj_last` | **Ladder boundaries**: `┼£^+ ┬╖ P_0 = 0` (top of ladder) and `┼£^- ┬╖ P_N = 0` (bottom). The first/last columns of `┼£^┬▒` vanish, so multiplication by `P_{0/N}` (which selects that column) gives `0`. Terminate the recursion at the highest- and lowest-weight projectors (╬▓-8 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderBoundary.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_isHermitian` / `sum_spinSDiagProj_eq_one` | **Hermiticity** of each `P_k`, and the **resolution of the identity** `╬ث_k P_k = 1`. The latter is the cornerstone for the matrix-unit decomposition: combined with the off-diagonal matrix units (╬▓-6, ╬▓-7), gives every matrix in `M_{N+1}(ظé)` (╬▓-9 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProjProperties.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_succ_eq_single` / `spinSOpMinus_mul_diagProj_eq_single` | **Off-diagonal matrix-unit decomposition**: `┼£^+ ┬╖ P_{i+1} = ظêأ((i+1)(Nظêْi)) ┬╖ E_{i, i+1}` and `┼£^- ┬╖ P_i = ظêأ((Nظêْi)(i+1)) ┬╖ E_{i+1, i}`, where `E_{a, b} := Matrix.single a b 1` is the (a, b) matrix unit (╬▓-10 of Issue #458). These are the simplest off-diagonal matrix units expressible via `┼£^┬▒` ladder action on diagonal projectors | `Quantum/SpinS/OffDiagUnit.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_sq_eq_diagonal` | `(┼£^{(3)})┬▓ = diag((N/2 ظêْ i)┬▓)`. Step toward the **Casimir identity** `(┼£^{(1)})┬▓ + (┼£^{(2)})┬▓ + (┼£^{(3)})┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬╖ 1` for general spin (╬▓-11 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Op3Square.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_spinSOpMinus_eq_diagonal` | `┼£^+ ┬╖ ┼£^- = diag((i + 1)(N ظêْ i))`. The product is diagonal because `┼£^+[i, l] ┬╖ ┼£^-[l, j]` is non-zero only when `l = i + 1 = j + 1`, forcing `i = j`. Combined with the analogous `┼£^- ┬╖ ┼£^+` and `(┼£^{(3)})┬▓`, this assembles the Casimir identity (╬▓-12 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PlusMinusDiag.lean` |
| `spinSOpMinus_mul_spinSOpPlus_eq_diagonal` | `┼£^- ┬╖ ┼£^+ = diag(i ┬╖ (N ظêْ i + 1))`. Symmetric to ╬▓-12 with `┼£^┬▒` swapped (╬▓-13 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/MinusPlusDiag.lean` |
| `spinSOp1_sq_add_spinSOp2_sq` / `spinSOp_total_squared` | **Casimir identity** for general spin-`S`: `(┼£^{(1)})┬▓ + (┼£^{(2)})┬▓ + (┼£^{(3)})┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬╖ 1`, equivalent to `S(S+1) ┬╖ 1` for `S = N/2`. Proves the irreducible spin-`S` representation has Casimir eigenvalue `S(S+1)` (Schur's lemma). The intermediate identity `(┼£^{(1)})┬▓ + (┼£^{(2)})┬▓ = (1/2)(┼£^+ ┬╖ ┼£^- + ┼£^- ┬╖ ┼£^+)` is proved using `module` (╬▓-14 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Casimir.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_mulVec_basis` | spin-`S` eigenstate equation `┼£^{(3)} ┬╖ \|kظار = (N/2 ظêْ k) ┬╖ \|kظار`, where `\|kظار := Pi.single k 1` is the `k`-th unit basis vector of `(Fin (N + 1) ظْ ظé)` (╬▓-15 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Eigenstates.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mulVec_basis` / `spinSOpMinus_mulVec_basis` | **Ladder action on basis vectors**: `┼£^+ ┬╖ \|kظار = ظêأ(k(N ظêْ k + 1)) ┬╖ \|k ظêْ 1ظار` for `k ظëح 1`, and `┼£^- ┬╖ \|kظار = ظêأ((N ظêْ k)(k + 1)) ┬╖ \|k + 1ظار` for `k ظëج N ظêْ 1`. The standard SU(2) ladder relations on the magnetic-quantum-number basis (╬▓-16 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderStates.lean` |
| `spinSOp_total_squared_mulVec_basis` | **Casimir eigenvalue on basis**: `(┼£)┬▓ ┬╖ \|kظار = (N(N+2)/4) ┬╖ \|kظار`. Direct consequence of `(┼£)┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬╖ 1` (╬▓-14) ظ¤ every basis vector has the universal Casimir eigenvalue, reflecting that the spin-`S` representation is a single irreducible (Schur) (╬▓-17 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirEigenvalue.lean` |
| `spinSOp{1,2,3,Plus,Minus}_commute_total_squared` | **Casimir invariance**: each `┼£^{(╬▒)}` and `┼£^┬▒` commutes with the Casimir `(┼£)┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬╖ 1`. Direct consequence of the scalar nature of the Casimir (╬▓-18 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirInvariance.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_commutator_spinSOpMinus` | **Third Cartan relation**: `[┼£^+, ┼£^-] = 2 ┬╖ ┼£^{(3)}`. Combines ╬▓-12 (`┼£^+ ┬╖ ┼£^- = diag((i + 1)(N ظêْ i))`) and ╬▓-13 (`┼£^- ┬╖ ┼£^+ = diag(i (N ظêْ i + 1))`); the difference `(i+1)(N-i) ظêْ i(N-i+1) = N ظêْ 2i = 2(N/2 ظêْ i)` matches `2 ┬╖ ┼£^{(3)}` (╬▓-19 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Cartan3.lean` |
| `spinSOp1_commutator_spinSOp2` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}] = i ┬╖ ┼£^{(3)}`. Derived from the Cartan relations by algebraic manipulation through `┼£^{(1)} = (┼£^+ + ┼£^-)/2` and `┼£^{(2)} = (┼£^+ ظêْ ┼£^-)/(2i)`: `(P+Q)(P-Q) ظêْ (P-Q)(P+Q) = -2[P, Q] = -4 ┼£^{(3)}`, then scalar simplification `-1/I = I` (╬▓-20 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator.lean` |
| `spinSOp2_commutator_spinSOp3` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}] = i ┬╖ ┼£^{(1)}`. Derived from Cartan ╬▓-2 (`[┼£^{(3)}, ┼£^┬▒] = ┬▒┼£^┬▒`) via `┼£^{(2)} = (┼£^+ ظêْ ┼£^-)/(2i)`: the commutator reduces to `(1/(2i)) (-┼£^+ ظêْ ┼£^-) = (-1/(2i)) ┬╖ 2 ┼£^{(1)} = i ┬╖ ┼£^{(1)}` (╬▓-21 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator23.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_commutator_spinSOp1` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[┼£^{(3)}, ┼£^{(1)}] = i ┬╖ ┼£^{(2)}`. Derived from Cartan ╬▓-2 via `┼£^{(1)} = (┼£^+ + ┼£^-)/2`: the commutator reduces to `(1/2) (┼£^+ ظêْ ┼£^-) = (1/2) ┬╖ (2i) ┬╖ ┼£^{(2)} = i ┬╖ ┼£^{(2)}`. Together with ╬▓-20 and ╬▓-21 this completes the standard SU(2) commutator algebra (Tasaki eq. (2.1.1)) for spin-`S` operators (╬▓-22 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator31.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_mul_self` / `spinSDiagProj_mul_of_ne` | **Idempotence and orthogonality** of the diagonal projectors `P_k = |kظارظاذk|`: `P_k ┬╖ P_k = P_k` and `P_i ┬╖ P_j = 0` for `i ظëب j`. Combined with ╬▓-9 (`ظêّ_k P_k = 1`) this gives the spectral decomposition of the identity for `┼£^{(3)}` (╬▓-23 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProjOrtho.lean` |
| `aeval_diagonal` | **Polynomial evaluation at a diagonal matrix**: `aeval (Matrix.diagonal v) p = Matrix.diagonal (fun i => p.eval (v i))`. Foundational lemma for the Lagrange-interpolation step (╬▓-25+) ظ¤ lets us pull a polynomial in `┼£^{(3)}` (a diagonal matrix) through to its scalar action on each diagonal entry. Proof by `Polynomial.induction_on'` on monomials and addition (╬▓-24 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/AevalDiagonal.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_eq_lagrange_aeval` | **Lagrange-interpolation formula for `P_k`**: `P_k = aeval (┼£^{(3)}) (Lagrange.basis Finset.univ (spinSOp3Eigen N) k)`, equivalently `P_k = ظê_{j ظëب k} (┼£^{(3)} ظêْ ╬╗_j ظت 1)/(╬╗_k ظêْ ╬╗_j)` with `╬╗_j = (N : ظé)/2 ظêْ j.val`. Combines ╬▓-24 (`aeval` of diagonal) with mathlib's `Lagrange.eval_basis_self` / `eval_basis_of_ne`. Each diagonal projector is therefore a **polynomial in `┼£^{(3)}`**, which combined with ╬▓-9 (`ظêّ P_k = 1`) gives an explicit polynomial decomposition of `1╠é` (╬▓-25 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LagrangeFormula.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_eq_one_add_I_smul_two` / `spinSOpMinus_eq_one_sub_I_smul_two` | **Inversion of the Cartesian definition**: `┼£^+ = ┼£^{(1)} + i ┬╖ ┼£^{(2)}` and `┼£^- = ┼£^{(1)} ظêْ i ┬╖ ┼£^{(2)}`. The defining identities `┼£^{(1)} = (1/2)(┼£^+ + ┼£^-)`, `┼£^{(2)} = (1/(2i))(┼£^+ ظêْ ┼£^-)` invert to express the ladder operators as **linear combinations** of the Hermitian Cartesian spin operators (╬▓-26 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PMAsOneTwo.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_mem_adjoin_spinSOp3` / `spinSDiagProj_mem_adjoin` | **Diagonal projectors live in the algebra generated by the spin operators**: `P_k ظêê Algebra.adjoin ظé {┼£^{(3)}}` (and a fortiori `P_k ظêê Algebra.adjoin ظé {┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}}`). Promotes the Lagrange-interpolation formula (╬▓-25) from "polynomial-evaluation" form to "subalgebra-membership" form via `Algebra.adjoin_singleton_eq_range_aeval` (╬▓-27 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/ProjMemAdjoin.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mem_adjoin` / `spinSOpMinus_mem_adjoin` | **Ladder operators live in `Algebra.adjoin ظé {┼£^{(╬▒)}}`**: `┼£^+, ┼£^- ظêê Algebra.adjoin ظé {┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}}`. Direct consequence of ╬▓-26 (`┼£^┬▒ = ┼£^{(1)} ┬▒ i ┬╖ ┼£^{(2)}`) and the fact that subalgebras are closed under `ظé`-linear combinations (╬▓-28 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PMMemAdjoin.lean` |
| `single_succ_mem_adjoin` / `single_succ_swap_mem_adjoin` | **Immediate-neighbor matrix units live in `Algebra.adjoin ظé {┼£^{(╬▒)}}`**: `E_{i, i+1}, E_{i+1, i} ظêê Algebra.adjoin ظé {┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}}`. Combines ╬▓-10 (`┼£^+ ┬╖ P_{i+1} = ظêأ((i+1)(N-i)) ┬╖ E_{i, i+1}`) with ╬▓-27 (`P_k ظêê adjoin`) and ╬▓-28 (`┼£^┬▒ ظêê adjoin`); the ladder coefficient is non-zero on the valid range and the subalgebra is closed under multiplication and scalar `smul`. Step toward expressing every matrix unit as a polynomial in `{┼£^{(╬▒)}}` (╬▓-29 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/NeighborUnitAdjoin.lean` |
| `single_offset_succ_mem_adjoin` / `single_offset_succ_swap_mem_adjoin` | **Arbitrary stride-(k+1) matrix units live in `Algebra.adjoin ظé {┼£^{(╬▒)}}`**: `E_{i, i+(k+1)}, E_{i+(k+1), i} ظêê Algebra.adjoin ظé {┼£^{(╬▒)}}` for any `k`. Induction on `k`: base case is ╬▓-29; inductive step chains via `Matrix.single_mul_single_same` (`E_{i,j} ┬╖ E_{j,k} = E_{i,k}`) (╬▓-30 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/OffsetUnitAdjoin.lean` |
| `matrix_single_mem_adjoin` | **Every matrix unit `E_{i,j}` lives in `Algebra.adjoin ظé {┼£^{(╬▒)}}`**. Three-case split: `i = j` reduces to ╬▓-27 via `Matrix.diagonal_single` (`E_{i,i} = P_i`); `i.val < j.val` is ╬▓-30 upper; `j.val < i.val` is ╬▓-30 lower. Last building block before the spanning theorem (╬▓-31 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllUnitsAdjoin.lean` |
| `matrix_mem_adjoin` / **`spinS_adjoin_eq_top`** | **≡ا» Tasaki ┬د2.1 Problem 2.1.a (P1d''') general-`S`**: every operator on the `(2S+1)`-dimensional spin-`S` Hilbert space `ظé^{N+1}` is a polynomial in `{1╠é, ┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}}`. Equivalently, `Algebra.adjoin ظé {┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}} = ظèج`. Proof: every matrix unit `E_{i,j} ظêê adjoin` (╬▓-31), so by the entry-wise decomposition `M = ظêّ_{i,j} M_{i,j} ظت E_{i,j}` (`Matrix.matrix_eq_sum_single`) and the linearity of the subalgebra, every matrix is in the adjoin (╬▓-32 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/SpanningTheorem.lean` |
| `ManyBodyOpS` / `onSiteS` / `spinSSiteOp{1,2,3,Plus,Minus}` | **Multi-site spin-`S` operator space** indexed by configurations `╧â : ╬ؤ ظْ Fin (N + 1)`, with the site-embedded operator `onSiteS i A` acting as `A` on site `i` and as the identity elsewhere; site-specialised `┼£_i^{(╬▒)}, ┼£_i^┬▒`. Hermiticity preservation `onSiteS_isHermitian` lifts from single-site to multi-site (Tasaki ┬د2.5 Phase B-╬▓ ╬▓-3a, Issue #412) | `Quantum/SpinS/MultiSiteCore.lean` + `Quantum/SpinS/MultiSite.lean` |

| `pauliX_eq_indicator` / `onSite_pauliX_mulVec_apply` | closed-form `pauliX a b = if b = 1 - a then 1 else 0`, lifted to `((onSite x ╧â^x).mulVec v) ╧ = v (siteFlipAt ╧ x)` for any state `v` (general extension of the basis-state action) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSign_prod_siteFlipAt` | `ظê_y ╬╡((siteFlipAt ╧ x) y) = -(ظê_y ╬╡(╧ y))` ظ¤ the per-site flip swaps `╬╡(╧ x)` with `╬╡(1 - ╧ x) = -╬╡(╧ x)`, flipping the total sign | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_pauliX_mulVec` | multi-site sign-flip equivariance for `╧â^x` (Tasaki ┬د2.3 (2.3.14) at ╬▒ = 1): `╬ء╠é_tot ((onSite x ╧â^x) v) = (-(onSite x ╧â^x))(╬ء╠é_tot v)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_pauliY_mulVec` | multi-site sign-flip equivariance for `╧â^y` (Tasaki ┬د2.3 (2.3.14) at ╬▒ = 2): `╬ء╠é_tot ((onSite x ╧â^y) v) = (-(onSite x ╧â^y))(╬ء╠é_tot v)`. The proof handles the per-site `┬▒i` factor via `conj(pauliY_sign(1 - s)) = pauliY_sign(s)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_add` / `_smul` / `_real_smul` | multi-spin `╬ء╠é_tot` is additive, antilinear in the scalar (`╬ء╠é_tot(c ظت v) = conj(c) ظت ╬ء╠é_tot v`), and real-linear (special case of antilinearity at real `r`) ظ¤ foundational for lifting Pauli-axis equivariance to bilinear / Heisenberg-type Hamiltonian forms | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_spinHalfOp{1,2,3}_mulVec` | Tasaki ┬د2.3 (2.3.14) for spin-1/2 ops `┼£^(╬▒) = ╧â^(╬▒) / 2`: `╬ء╠é_tot ((onSite x ┼£^(╬▒)) v) = (-(onSite x ┼£^(╬▒)))(╬ء╠é_tot v)` for ╬▒ = 1, 2, 3 ظ¤ direct corollaries of the Pauli versions by scalar (1/2) multiplication | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_spinHalfDot_mulVec` | **Time-reversal invariance of the bilinear `┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y`** (Tasaki ┬د2.3): `╬ء╠é_tot ((┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y) v) = (┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y)(╬ء╠é_tot v)` ظ¤ two equivariance `-1` factors cancel; sums per-axis | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec` | **Time-reversal invariance of the Heisenberg Hamiltonian** (Tasaki ┬د2.3): for real coupling `J` (`conj(J(x,y)) = J(x,y)`), `╬ء╠é_tot ((H J) v) = (H J)(╬ء╠é_tot v)`. Combines per-bond invariance + ╬ء╠é_tot antilinearity (J reality) + additivity (over double-sum) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_conj` / `periodicChainCoupling_conj` | every entry of `openChainCoupling N J` (resp. `periodicChainCoupling N J`) is real (under complex conjugation), since `J : ظإ` makes `(-(J : ظé))` real-valued | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_openChainHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_periodicChainHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_squareLatticeHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_squareTorusHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_cubicLatticeHeisenberg_mulVec` | concrete time-reversal invariance: the open / periodic chain, the 2D open square / torus, and the 3D cubic Heisenberg Hamiltonians all commute with `╬ء╠é_tot` for any real coupling `J : ظإ`. Backed by `*Coupling_conj` reality lemmas in `HeisenbergChain.lean` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_basisVec_upDown` / `_basisVec_basisSwap_upDown` | `╬ء╠é_tot |ظّظôظار = -|ظôظّظار` and `╬ء╠é_tot |ظôظّظار = -|ظّظôظار` on `Fin 2` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_singlet` | the two-site spin singlet `|ظّظôظار - |ظôظّظار` is **time-reversal invariant** (Tasaki ┬د2.3 / ┬دA.3): being the SU(2) `S = 0` representation, it survives `╬ء╠é_tot` unchanged | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_triplet_zero` | the triplet `m = 0` state `|ظّظôظار + |ظôظّظار` is **anti-invariant** under `╬ء╠é_tot`: `╬ء╠é_tot (|ظّظôظار + |ظôظّظار) = -(|ظّظôظار + |ظôظّظار)` (the symmetric combination picks up a minus sign from the per-basis-vector flip) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |

### Multi-body operator space (abstract lattice)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬د2.2, pp. 21-26 (tensor-product Hilbert space and site-local
operators). The lattice `╬ؤ` is an arbitrary finite set with decidable
equality; specializing to `╬ؤ = Fin N` recovers an `N`-site chain.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `ManyBodyOp ╬ؤ` | `Matrix (╬ؤ ظْ Fin 2) (╬ؤ ظْ Fin 2) ظé` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite i A` | site-embedded operator at `i : ╬ؤ` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_isHermitian` | `A.IsHermitian ظْ (onSite i A).IsHermitian` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_add`, `onSite_sub`, `onSite_zero`, `onSite_smul`, `onSite_one` | linearity of the site embedding and `onSite i 1 = 1` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_of_ne` | distinct-site commutation (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x ظëب y`, S = 1/2) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `basisVec` / `onSite_mulVec_basisVec` | tensor-product basis states and their action under site operators (Tasaki (2.2.1)/(2.2.4)) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_same` | same-site product `onSite i A ┬╖ onSite i B = onSite i (A ┬╖ B)` (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x = y`) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_commutator_same` | same-site commutator `[onSite i A, onSite i B] = onSite i [A, B]` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.mul_of_commute` | commuting Hermitians multiply Hermitian (generic; relocated to the matrix-analysis layer in PR #4342) | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |

### Generic matrix-analysis helpers (`Math/MatrixAnalysis/`)

Topic-organized generic linear-algebra facts extracted from the physics files where they had been
re-proved as private helpers (Issue [#4339](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4339)).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `Matrix.isHermitian_sum` | a finite sum of Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.mul_of_commute` | the product of two commuting Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_isHermitian` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_sq_of_sq_one` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting involutions is an involution | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_mul_self_of_idempotent` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting idempotents is idempotent | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_mulVec_eq_self` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of matrices each fixing `╧ê` also fixes `╧ê` | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.trace_im` | the trace of a Hermitian matrix is real | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_star_of_isHermitian` | `Tr(A┬╖B)` of two Hermitian matrices is conjugation-invariant | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |

(The two trace helpers above are also listed in the Gibbs-state section where they are consumed; the
canonical definitions live in `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean`.)

These are consumed by the JordanظôWigner string / Hubbard hard-core projection layers (PR #4342),
replacing the per-file private copies in `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean`,
`Fermion/JordanWigner/Operators.lean`, and `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean`.

### Horschظôvon der Linden low-lying states (Tasaki ┬د3.4, Theorem 3.1)

Start of the Chapters 3ظô10 backfill (Issue
[#4485](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4485); book order, infinite systems in
scope). Tasaki ┬د3.4, Theorem 3.1, eqs. (3.4.7)ظô(3.4.12), pp. 66ظô67.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `horsch_vonderLinden_lowLying` | for Hermitian `H` with min eigenvalue `Eظé = eigenvalues iظé`, a unit state `╬ô` orthogonal to the ground eigenvector with `ظاذ╬ô,H╬ôظار ظëج Eظé+╬┤` yields an energy eigenstate `j ظëب iظé` with `Eظé ظëج E_j ظëج Eظé+╬┤` (a low-lying state; possibly another ground state if degenerate, as Tasaki notes) | `Quantum/HorschVonderLinden.lean` |
| `kaplan_horsch_vonderLinden_order_lower_bound` | **Theorem 3.2, finite-volume core** (┬د3.4; the thermodynamic double limit 3.4.22 is not formalized): for the field-perturbed ground state `╬ذ` of `H ظêْ h┬╖O` (`h>0`) and any trial `╬ئ`, the order parameter obeys `ظاذ╬ئ,O╬ئظار + (Eظéظêْظاذ╬ئ,H╬ئظار)/h ظëج ظاذ╬ذ,O╬ذظار` (eq. (3.4.21), the variational core; the double limit `Lظّظêئ,hظô0` gives the ظêأqظé SSB bound) | `Quantum/KaplanHorschVonderLinden.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS` / `staggeredOrderOpS_isHermitian` | **Theorem 4.1** (┬د4.1, DysonظôLiebظôSimon, toward; eq. (4.1.7)): the staggered (N├رel) order operator `├¤ = ╬ث_x ╬╡_x ┼£_x^(3)` (`╬╡_x = ┬▒1` sublattice sign) and its Hermiticity (real ┬▒1 combination of Hermitian `┼£_x^(3)`), so `ظاذ╬خ,├¤┬▓╬خظار` is a real order parameter. The N├رel-LRO statement needs the actual hypercubic torus + reflection positivity (false for arbitrary bipartite graphs); deferred pending that infrastructure (infinite-volume in scope) | `Quantum/SpinS/DysonLiebSimon.lean` |
| `shastry_no_symmetry_breaking_1d` | **Theorem 4.2** (┬د4.1, Shastry, AXIOM; eqs. (4.1.9)ظô(4.1.10)): absence of SSB in 1D. For the 1D AFM Heisenberg ring under a staggered field `─ج_h = ╬ث_x ┼£_x┬╖┼£_{x+1} ظêْ h┬╖├¤_L^(3)`, the per-site staggered order parameter of any normalized ground state vanishes in the iterated limit `lim_{hظô0} lim_{Lظّظêئ}` (eq. (4.1.10), ╬╡ظô╬┤ form). Tasaki states it without proof; the deep argument is now being formalized as the **reflection-positivity infrastructure project** (#4777, codex-validated 11-PR plan). Defines `ringCoupling`, `ringStaggeredSublattice`, `staggeredFieldChainHamiltonianS`. **RP infra layer 1 (in progress):** `RingBondReflection.lean` ظ¤ even-ring bond reflection `ringReflect n x = 2nظêْ1ظêْx` (involutive, half-swap, staggered-sign flip); `RingReflectionTheta.lean` ظ¤ reflection map `╬╕(A) ╧â ╧ = conj(A(╧╧â)(╧╧))`, an antilinear `*`-automorphism, with the single-site bridge `╬╕(onSiteS x A) = onSiteS (ringReflect n x) (conj A)`. **RP infra layer 2** (`RingReflectionHamiltonian.lean`): the Hamiltonian ╬╕-decomposition ظ¤ orientation reversal `J(r x)(r y)=J y x`, spin-op conjugation (S┬╣/S┬│ real, S┬▓ imaginary), `╬╕(┼£_x┬╖┼£_y)=┼£_{r x}┬╖┼£_{r y}`, `╬╕(├¤_L^(3))=ظêْ├¤_L^(3)` (staggered-sign flip), `╬╕(─ج)=─ج` (reindex + adjacency + dot-comm), and `╬╕(─ج_h)=─ج_{ظêْh}` (the reflection symmetry exchanging the two staggered ground states). **RP infra layer 3** (`RingReflectionPositivity.lean`): the left-half subalgebra `SupportedOnLeftS` = `B(H_left) ظèù I_right` (two conditions: entries vanish off the right-diagonal **and** are independent of the common right-half value) with closure (zero/one/add/smul, left-site `onSiteS`), `╬╕` maps left-supported to right-supported (`theta_right`), and the reflection-positive functional predicate `ReflectionPositiveFunctionalS` (`0 ظëج Re ╧(╬╕(A)┬╖A)` for left-supported `A`). **RP infra layer 4** (`RingReflectionTraceCone.lean`): the `╬▓ = 0` base case ظ¤ `traceFunctional_reflectionPositive` proves the trace functional `X ظخ Tr X` is reflection positive, i.e. `0 ظëج Re Tr(╬╕(A)┬╖A)` for every left-supported `A`. The proof collapses `Tr(╬╕(A)┬╖A) = ظêّ_{╧â,╬╝} ╬╕(A)╧â╬╝┬╖A╬╝╧â` to its diagonal (both support conditions force `╬╝=╧â`), then factorizes over the left/right configuration split (`configSplitEquiv`, from `finSumFinEquiv`): the diagonal value `A ╧â ╧â` depends only on the left half, giving `Tr(╬╕(A)┬╖A) = conj S ┬╖ S = ظûSظû┬▓ ظëح 0` with `S = ظêّ_ظô D ظô`. This is the infinite-temperature trace cone on which a later layer mounts the Gibbs exponential via Trotter. **RP infra layer 5** (`RingReflectionWeightedCone.lean`): the algebraic completion of the cone ظ¤ `SupportedOnLeftS.mul` (the left-half subalgebra is closed under products), `SupportedOnLeftS.mul_theta_comm` (a left-supported `A` commutes with `╬╕(B)`, which acts on the right half), and the **weighted trace cone** `weightedTraceFunctional_reflectionPositive` (`X ظخ Tr((╬╕(C)┬╖C)┬╖X)` is reflection positive for left-supported `C`, via `Tr((╬╕C┬╖C)┬╖(╬╕A┬╖A)) = Tr(╬╕(C┬╖A)┬╖(C┬╖A)) ظëح 0`) plus its nonnegative-finite-combination version `weightedTraceFunctional_reflectionPositive_finsetSum` ظ¤ the cone on which the Trotter/Lie-product factors of the Gibbs exponential will be mounted. **RP infra layer 6** (`RingReflectionGibbsCone.lean`): the reflection-positive trace-weight cone ظ¤ `RPTraceWeightS M` (`X ظخ Tr(M┬╖X)` is an RP functional), the cone-representability predicate `RPTraceConeRepS` (nonnegative finite combination `ظêّ cط╡ت ╬╕(Cط╡ت)┬╖Cط╡ت`) with closure under `one`/`zero`/`add`/`smul_nonneg`/`mul`/`pow` (the product closure via `weightGen_mul`: `(╬╕C┬╖C)(╬╕D┬╖D) = ╬╕(CD)┬╖(CD)`), `RPTraceConeRepS.rpTraceWeight` (representable ظا╣ RP trace weight, from the weighted cone), and `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto` (RP trace weights are closed under limits, by finite-dimensional trace continuity) ظ¤ the cone on which the next layer mounts the interaction Gibbs exponential `exp(t┬╖ظêّcط╡ت╬╕(Cط╡ت)┬╖Cط╡ت)` as a limit of cone-representable partial sums. **RP infra layer 7** (`RingReflectionGibbsExp.lean`): `rpInteractionExp_reflectionPositive` ظ¤ for a nonnegative finite interaction `D = t┬╖ظêّط╡ت cط╡ت ╬╕(Cط╡ت)┬╖Cط╡ت` (`t, cط╡ت ظëح 0`, `Cط╡ت` left-supported), the matrix exponential `exp D` is a reflection-positive trace weight. Each partial sum `ظêّ_{k<m}(k!)ظ╗┬╣Dط╡` of the exponential series is cone-representable (`smul_nonneg`/`add`/`pow`), hence an RP trace weight, and `exp D` is their limit (`expSeries_summable'.hasSum.tendsto_sum_nat`), so `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto` applies. Uses the `Lظêئ`-operator-norm Banach structure (`open scoped Matrix.Norms.Operator`), whose topology is the entrywise (Pi) topology ظ¤ the same one underlying the trace continuity, so no topology diamond. **RP infra layer 8** (`RingReflectionExpSupport.lean`): the matrix exponential and the left subalgebra / reflection map ظ¤ `SupportedOnLeftS.exp` (`X` left-supported ظا╣ `exp X` left-supported: the left subalgebra is closed under products/sums/scalars/entrywise-limits via `SupportedOnLeftS.of_tendsto`, and `exp X` is the limit of its partial sums) and `ringReflectionThetaS_exp` (`╬╕(exp X) = exp(╬╕ X)`: `╬╕` is a continuous conjugate-linear `*`-automorphism with real exponential coefficients) ظ¤ the building blocks for the `e^{-╬▓H_L}` Hamiltonian factor of the full Gibbs reflection-positivity decomposition | `Quantum/SpinS/ShastryNoSSB.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingBondReflection.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTheta.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionHamiltonian.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionPositivity.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTraceCone.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionWeightedCone.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsCone.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsExp.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionExpSupport.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRPDecomposition.lean` |
| `falkBruch_double_commutator` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkظôBruch** (`FalkBruchDoubleCommutator.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the concrete ground-state FalkظôBruch inequality ظ¤ for a ground state `╬خ`, Hermitian `A` and a potential `y` for `A╬خ` (`(HظêْEظé)y = A╬خ`), `2(Reظاذ╬خ,A┬▓╬خظار)┬▓ ظëج Reظاذ╬خ,[A,[H,A]]╬خظار┬╖Reظاذy,A╬خظار`; combines the abstract FalkظôBruch (`K = HظêْEظé` PSD) with the double-commutator = variational-energy identity | `Quantum/SpinS/FalkBruchDoubleCommutator.lean` |
| `hermitianSubMin_posSemidef` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkظôBruch** (`HermitianSubMinPosSemidef.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the Hamiltonian shifted to its ground energy `H ظêْ Eظé` (`Eظé = hermitianMinEigenvalue`) is positive-semidefinite ظ¤ the quadratic form is real (Hermiticity, `isHermitian_dotProduct_mulVec_im_zero`) with nonnegative real part (the variational lower bound); this is the PSD `K` fed into the FalkظôBruch inequality | `Quantum/SpinS/HermitianSubMinPosSemidef.lean` |
| `falkBruch_of_mulVec_eq` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkظôBruch** (`FalkBruch.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the abstract ground-state FalkظôBruch inequality ظ¤ for PSD `K` and `w` in the range of `K` (`K y = w`), `(Reظاذw,wظار)┬▓ ظëج Reظاذw,K wظار┬╖Reظاذy,wظار`, an immediate instance of the PSD CauchyظôSchwarz (`posSemidef_re_dotProduct_mulVec_sq_le`) with second argument `y`; no spectral theory / pseudo-inverse needed | `Quantum/SpinS/FalkBruch.lean` |
| `double_commutator_ground_state_nonneg` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkظôBruch** (`DoubleCommutatorNonneg.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the ground-state double commutator `Reظاذ╬خ, [A,[H,A]] ╬خظار ظëح 0` for Hermitian `A` and a minimal-eigenvalue eigenvector `╬خ` ظ¤ it equals twice the variational energy of `A╬خ`, nonnegative since the ground energy is the Rayleigh minimum (`hermitianMinEigenvalue_mul_dotProduct_re_le_rayleighOnVec`); the sign of the FalkظôBruch numerator | `Quantum/SpinS/DoubleCommutatorNonneg.lean` |
| `double_commutator_ground_state_eq` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkظôBruch** (`DoubleCommutatorVariational.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): for Hermitian `H`, `A` and a ground state `H╬خ = Eظé╬خ` (`Eظé` real), the f-sum-rule oscillator strength equals twice the variational energy of `A╬خ` ظ¤ `ظاذ╬خ, [A,[H,A]] ╬خظار = 2ظاذA╬خ, H A╬خظار ظêْ 2EظéظاذA╬خ, A╬خظار` (Hermitian-shift); the denominator of the ground-state FalkظôBruch inequality | `Quantum/SpinS/DoubleCommutatorVariational.lean` |
| `spinSDot_double_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`SingleBondDoubleCommutator.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the per-bond double commutator is the negated transverse bond energy `[┼£_a^{(3)}, [┼£_a┬╖┼£_b, ┼£_a^{(3)}]] = ظêْ(┼£_a^{(1)}┼£_b^{(1)} + ┼£_a^{(2)}┼£_b^{(2)})` (the f-sum-rule integrand / oscillator strength of the staggered mode), bounded by the bond energy | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleBondDoubleCommutator.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_commutator_onSiteS` | **Cor 4.3 / IR bound** (`HeisenbergSpinSOp3Commutator.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the Heisenberg Hamiltonian commutator distributes over bonds `[─ج_J, ┼£_z^{(3)}] = ╬ث_x ╬ث_y J_{xy} [┼£_x┬╖┼£_y, ┼£_z^{(3)}]`; with the per-bond case analysis this reduces the spin-current divergence `[─ج, ┼£_z^{(3)}]` to the two bonds incident to `z` | `Quantum/SpinS/HeisenbergSpinSOp3Commutator.lean` |
| `spinSDot_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3` / `_right` | **Cor 4.3 / IR bound** (`SingleBondSpinSOp3Commutator.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the single-bond spin current `[┼£_a┬╖┼£_b, ┼£_a^{(3)}] = i(┼£_a^{(1)} ┼£_b^{(2)} ظêْ ┼£_a^{(2)} ┼£_b^{(1)})` for `a ظëب b` (only the transverse bond components fail to commute with `┼£_a^{(3)}`, via the cyclic SU(2) commutators; `_right` is the symmetric right-endpoint companion `[┼£_a┬╖┼£_b, ┼£_b^{(3)}]`, and `_eq_zero_of_ne` is the off-bond vanishing `[┼£_x┬╖┼£_y, ┼£_z^{(3)}] = 0` for `z ظêë {x,y}`); the building block of `[─ج, ┼£_z^{(3)}]` and the double commutator in the infrared / f-sum-rule bound | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleBondSpinSOp3Commutator.lean` |
| `expectation_abs_le_manyBodyOperatorNormS` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`ExpectationNormBound.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the general norm-to-expectation bound ظ¤ for any `M` and a normalized state `╬خ` (`star ╬خ ظشإط╡ح ╬خ = 1`), `|ظاذ╬خ, M ╬خظار.re| ظëج ظûMظû` (normalized-state form of `abs_re_dotProduct_mulVec_le_norm_mul`); converts operator-norm bounds into expectation bounds in the FalkظôBruch argument | `Quantum/SpinS/ExpectationNormBound.lean` |
| `transverseBondEnergy_expectation_abs_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`TransverseBondEnergyExpectation.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): in a normalized state `╬خ`, the transverse bond energy expectation `|ظاذ╬خ, (┼£_a^{(1)}┼£_b^{(1)} + ┼£_a^{(2)}┼£_b^{(2)}) ╬خظار.re| ظëج 2N┬▓` (the operator-norm bound + the normظْexpectation bridge `abs_re_dotProduct_mulVec_le_norm_mul` + `ظû╬خظû = 1`); summed over the `O(L)` adjacent bonds bounds the oscillator strength by `O(L)` | `Quantum/SpinS/TransverseBondEnergyExpectation.lean` |
| `spinSDot_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3_norm_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`SingleBondCommutatorNorm.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the single-bond spin current `[┼£_a┬╖┼£_b, ┼£_a^{(3)}]` has operator norm `ظëج 2N┬▓` (`ظûiظû = 1` + per-site norm bounds + submultiplicativity); the `O(1)` bound per bond, summed over `z`'s two incident bonds, bounds `ظû[─ج, ┼£_z^{(3)}]ظû` by `O(1)` | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleBondCommutatorNorm.lean` |
| `transverseBondEnergy_manyBodyOperatorNormS_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`TransverseBondEnergyNorm.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the transverse bond energy (per-bond oscillator strength `= ظêْ[┼£_a^{(3)},[┼£_a┬╖┼£_b,┼£_a^{(3)}]]`) has operator norm `ظû┼£_a^{(1)}┼£_b^{(1)} + ┼£_a^{(2)}┼£_b^{(2)}ظû ظëج 2N┬▓` (per-site norm bound `ظû┼£^{(╬▒)}ظû ظëج N` + submultiplicativity); summed over the `O(L)` adjacent bonds this bounds the oscillator strength by `O(L)` | `Quantum/SpinS/TransverseBondEnergyNorm.lean` |
| `pair_double_commutator_eq_zero_of_ne` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`PairCommutatorVanish.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): off-pair vanishing `[┼£_x^{(3)}, [─ج, ┼£_z^{(3)}]] = 0` for `x ظêë {zظêْ1, z, z+1}` ظ¤ since `[─ج, ┼£_z^{(3)}]` is supported on `z`'s two incident bonds, `┼£_x^{(3)}` commutes with it (Commute lemmas); hence the oscillator-strength double sum has only `O(L)` nonzero terms (three per `z`) | `Quantum/SpinS/PairCommutatorVanish.lean` |
| `no_long_range_order_1d_of_susceptibility` | **Cor 4.3 / conditional reduction** (`NoLongRangeOrderConditional.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the exact `╬╡`ظô`╬┤` statement of Corollary 4.3 *modulo the susceptibility bound* ظ¤ if there is `C ظëح 0` such that every normalized ground state of the zero-field ring has a potential `y` for `├¤╬خ` with `Reظاذy,├¤╬خظار ظëج C┬╖L`, then for every `╬╡ > 0` there is `Lظé` beyond which every normalized ground state has `\|ظاذ╬خ,├¤┬▓╬خظار.re/L┬▓\| < ╬╡` (assembling the `O(L)` oscillator bound + susceptibility reduction + ground-state bridge + an Archimedean `╬╡`ظô`╬┤`). This isolates the unconditional Cor 4.3 to the reflection-positivity infrared bound `Reظاذy,├¤╬خظار ظëج C┬╖L`; the axiom `no_long_range_order_1d` remains until that bound is proven (PR #4848) | `Quantum/SpinS/NoLongRangeOrderConditional.lean` |
| `groundState_mulVec_eq_hermitianMinEigenvalue` | **Cor 4.3 / ground-state bridge** (`HermitianGroundStateEigenvalue.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): a ground-state eigenvalue equals the minimum eigenvalue ظ¤ for a Hermitian `H` and a normalized eigenvector `╬خ` (`H╬خ = Eظéظت╬خ`) whose `Eظé` has minimal real part over all eigenpairs, `H╬خ = (hermitianMinEigenvalue H)ظت╬خ` (`Eظé.im = 0` by Hermiticity; `Eظé.re` squeezed between the variational lower bound and the minimizing eigenvector); bridges the "energy-minimizing eigenpair" axiom hypothesis to the FalkظôBruch `hermitianMinEigenvalue` interface (PR #4847) | `Quantum/SpinS/HermitianGroundStateEigenvalue.lean` |
| `staggeredOrder_sq_le_susceptibility` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkظôBruch** (`StaggeredOrderSusceptibility.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the squared staggered order parameter is bounded by `O(L)` times the susceptibility ظ¤ for a ground state `╬خ` of the zero-field ring (eigenvalue `hermitianMinEigenvalue`) and a potential `y` for `├¤╬خ` (`(─جظêْEظé)y = ├¤╬خ`), `2(Reظاذ╬خ,├¤┬▓╬خظار)┬▓ ظëج 12N┬│┬╖L┬╖Reظاذy,├¤╬خظار` (FalkظôBruch `2ظاذ├¤┬▓ظار┬▓ظëجosc┬╖╧ç` with the `O(L)` oscillator bound `oscظëج12N┬│L` and `╧ç=Reظاذy,├¤╬خظارظëح0` PSD); reduces Cor 4.3 to the susceptibility bound `╧ç ظëج C┬╖L` (PR #4846) | `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredOrderSusceptibility.lean` |
| `oscillatorStrength_abs_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`OscillatorStrengthBound.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the f-sum-rule oscillator strength is `O(L)` ظ¤ in a normalized state `╬خ`, `|ظاذ╬خ, [├¤, [─ج, ├¤]] ╬خظار.re| ظëج 12N┬│┬╖L` (assembling the double-sum form, the off-pair vanishing restricting to three nonzero `z` per `x`, the uniform per-pair norm `ظëج 4N┬│`, and the normظْexpectation bridge); the `O(L)` numerator of the ground-state FalkظôBruch bound (PR #4845) | `Quantum/SpinS/OscillatorStrengthBound.lean` |
| `pair_double_commutator_norm_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`PairCommutatorNorm.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): each summand of the oscillator strength is bounded uniformly in `L` ظ¤ `ظû[┼£_x^{(3)}, [─ج, ┼£_z^{(3)}]]ظû ظëج 4N┬│` (commutator norm `ظëج 2ظû┼£^{(3)}ظû┬╖ظû[─ج, ┼£_z^{(3)}]ظû` with `ظû┼£^{(3)}ظû ظëج N/2` and `ظû[─ج, ┼£_z^{(3)}]ظû ظëج 4N┬▓`); combined with off-pair vanishing gives `O(L)` | `Quantum/SpinS/PairCommutatorNorm.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3_norm_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`RingHamiltonianCommutatorNorm.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): since `[─ج, ┼£_z^{(3)}]` localizes to `z`'s two incident bonds, its operator norm is `O(1)` ظ¤ `ظû[─ج, ┼£_z^{(3)}]ظû ظëج 4N┬▓` (sum of the two single-bond spin-current norms `ظëج 2N┬▓` each); the length-independent locality making the oscillator strength `O(L)` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingHamiltonianCommutatorNorm.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3_closed` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`RingHamiltonianCommutatorClosed.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): closed form of the spin-current divergence on a ring `L ظëح 2` ظ¤ `[─ج, ┼£_z^{(3)}] = [┼£_z┬╖┼£_{z+1}, ┼£_z^{(3)}] + [┼£_{zظêْ1}┬╖┼£_z, ┼£_z^{(3)}]` (the bond sum collapses to `z`'s two incident bonds via `Finset.sum_pair`, every other bond commuting); plus `finRotate_ne_self` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingHamiltonianCommutatorClosed.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`RingHamiltonianCommutatorBondSum.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the ring Hamiltonian commutator with `┼£_z^{(3)}` as a bond sum `[─ج, ┼£_z^{(3)}] = ╬ث_x [┼£_x┬╖┼£_{x+1}, ┼£_z^{(3)}]` (bond-sum form + commutator distribution); since each bond commutator vanishes off `z`, this localizes the spin-current divergence to `z`'s two incident bonds | `Quantum/SpinS/RingHamiltonianCommutatorBondSum.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_eq_bondSum_general` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`RingBondSumGeneral.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian on `Fin L` of any length as a bond sum `─ج = ╬ث_x ┼£_x┬╖┼£_{finRotate L x}` (directed coupling collapses the defining double sum); the form used to localize `[─ج, ┼£_z^{(3)}]` to the two bonds incident to `z` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingBondSumGeneral.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS_double_commutator_dotProduct` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`OscillatorStrengthForm.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the oscillator strength as a double sum of two-site expectations `ظاذ╬خ,[├¤,[─ج,├¤]]╬خظار = ╬ث_x ╬ث_z ╬╡_x ╬╡_z ظاذ╬خ,[┼£_x^{(3)},[─ج,┼£_z^{(3)}]]╬خظار` (mulVec/dotProduct linearity over the operator double sum), whose summands vanish off adjacent sites ظ¤ the `O(L)` oscillator strength | `Quantum/SpinS/OscillatorStrengthForm.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS_double_commutator` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`StaggeredOrderDoubleCommutator.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the f-sum-rule double commutator as a double site sum `[├¤,[─ج,├¤]] = ╬ث_x ╬ث_z ╬╡_x ╬╡_z [┼£_x^{(3)},[─ج,┼£_z^{(3)}]]` (order-operator commutator distribution applied twice; `_commutator'` reversed variant), whose summands vanish off adjacent sites so the apparent `O(L┬▓)` collapses to `O(L)` | `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredOrderDoubleCommutator.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS_commutator` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`StaggeredOrderCommutator.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the staggered order operator commutator distributes over sites `[├¤, C] = ╬ث_x ╬╡_x [┼£_x^{(3)}, C]`; applied twice it expands the f-sum-rule double commutator `[├¤,[─ج,├¤]]` into a double site sum, whose terms vanish off adjacent sites (reducing the naive `O(L┬▓)` count to `O(L)`) | `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredOrderCommutator.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS_sq_dotProduct` | **Cor 4.3 bridge** (`StaggeredOrderSquareForm.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the ground-state quadratic-form expansion `ظاذ╬خ, ├¤┬▓ ╬خظار = ╬ث_x ╬ث_y ╬╡_x ╬╡_y ظاذ╬خ, ┼£_x^{(3)} ┼£_y^{(3)} ╬خظار`, rewriting the order parameter in the exact form `star ╬خ ظشإط╡ح (├¤┬▓).mulVec ╬خ` of the Corollary through the two-point functions the RP machinery bounds | `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredOrderSquareForm.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS_sq_eq_sum` | **Cor 4.3 bridge** (`StaggeredOrderSquare.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the staggered order operator squared expands as the two-point double sum `├¤┬▓ = ╬ث_x ╬ث_y ╬╡_x ╬╡_y ┼£_x^{(3)} ┼£_y^{(3)}` ظ¤ the bridge from the order parameter to the two-point functions bounded by the reflection-positivity / infrared machinery (the squared order parameter per `L┬▓` is controlled by the total two-point sum, only `O(L)` in one dimension) | `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredOrderSquare.lean` |
| `rpTraceWeight_cauchySchwarz_abs` | **RP infra layer 40** (`RingReflectionCauchySchwarzSqrt.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the square-root (usable) form of the RP CauchyظôSchwarz ظ¤ `|Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖B)| ظëج ظêأ(Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖A))┬╖ظêأ(Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕B┬╖B))` for a reflection-invariant RP trace weight `M` and left-supported `A,B`, by taking roots in the chessboard inequality (diagonals nonnegative); the form iterated in the DLS spreading argument | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionCauchySchwarzSqrt.lean` |
| `ringTranslateConj` / `chessboard_ringTranslateConj_of` | **RP infra layer 39** (`RingReflectionChessboardTransport.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): conjugation by the ring translation `╧(X) = Tظب┬╖X┬╖T` is a trace-preserving `*`-homomorphism (`ringTranslateConj_mul`/`_trace`), so any chessboard estimate transports unchanged to the translation-conjugated data `(╧M, ╧(╬╕A), ╧B)` (`chessboard_ringTranslateConj_of`) ظ¤ the weight `╧M` is the Marshall gauge relative to the shifted reflection plane; carries the single-axis chessboard to every bond toward Gaussian domination | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionChessboardTransport.lean` |
| `chainTranslation_conj_gibbs` / `thermalAverageReS_chainTranslation` | **RP infra layer 38** (`RingTranslationGibbs.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): unlike the half-system gauge, the physical ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian is translation invariant (`chainTranslation_conj_heisenbergHamiltonianS`), so the physical Gibbs operator `exp(ظêْ╬▓┬╖─ج)` is translation invariant (`chainTranslation_conj_gibbs`) and every physical thermal average is translation covariant `ظاذTظبATظار = ظاذAظار` (`thermalAverageReS_chainTranslation`); the thermal-state translation symmetry for the momentum-space infrared analysis | `Quantum/SpinS/RingTranslationGibbs.lean` |
| `chainTranslation_conj_ringReflectionThetaS_onSiteS` / `_spinSDot` | **RP infra layer 37** (`RingReflectionTranslation.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): translation covariance of the fixed-axis reflection map `╬╕` ظ¤ conjugating `╬╕(onSiteS x A)` (resp. `╬╕(┼£_x┬╖┼£_y)`) by the ring translation `chainTranslationOp` shifts the reflection axis, sending the sites to `finRotate (ringReflect x)` (resp. both endpoints); the foundation for reflections across every bond, needed to spread the single-axis chessboard toward DLS Gaussian domination | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTranslation.lean` |
| `ringDLSGibbs_reflection_step` | **RP infra layer 36** (`RingReflectionGibbsChessboard.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the concrete chessboard inequality for the gauged ring Gibbs weight ظ¤ combining its reflection positivity (Gibbs capstone) and reflection invariance (`╬╕M=M`), for `╬▓ ظëح 0` and left-supported `A,B`, `(Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖B))┬▓ ظëج Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖A)┬╖Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕B┬╖B)` with `M = exp(ظêْ╬▓┬╖toHamiltonian)`; the DLS factorization estimate for the thermal state of the physical ring antiferromagnet | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsChessboard.lean` |
| `ringReflectionThetaS_toHamiltonian` / `_ringDLSGibbs` | **RP infra layer 35** (`RingReflectionThetaInvariance.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the gauged DLS Hamiltonian is reflection-invariant `╬╕(toHamiltonian) = toHamiltonian` (the `H_L + ╬╕(H_L)` part is swapped to itself; each crossing bond `╬╕(crossBondInteractionS x) = crossBondInteractionS x` since `┼£_x^╬▒` commutes with its right-half reflection `╬╕(┼£_x^╬▒)`), hence the gauged ring Gibbs weight `exp(ظêْ╬▓┬╖toHamiltonian)` is reflection-invariant ظ¤ supplying the `╬╕M = M` hypothesis for the chessboard/real-diagonal lemmas | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionThetaInvariance.lean` |
| `rpForm_re_symm` / `rpTraceWeight_reflection_step` | **RP infra layer 34** (`RingReflectionChessboard.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): for a reflection-invariant RP trace weight `M` (`╬╕M=M`) and left-supported `A,B`, the off-diagonal form value is symmetric `Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖B) = Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕B┬╖A)` (since `A` commutes with the right-half `╬╕B`); feeding this into the CauchyظôSchwarz discriminant collapses the cross term, giving the chessboard bound `(Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖B))┬▓ ظëج Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖A)┬╖Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕B┬╖B)` ظ¤ the DLS factorization step underlying Gaussian domination | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionChessboard.lean` |
| `ringReflectionThetaS_trace` / `rpForm_diag_im_zero` | **RP infra layer 33** (`RingReflectionTraceReality.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the reflection map conjugates the trace `Tr(╬╕X) = conj(Tr X)` (reindex the diagonal by the involutive site reflection), so for a reflection-invariant weight `M` (`╬╕M = M`) and left-supported `A` the RP-form diagonal `Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖A)` is real (`╬╕┬▓=id`, `*`-homomorphism, `A` commutes with `╬╕A`); together with the CauchyظôSchwarz this makes `(A,B)ظخTr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖B)` a genuine positive-semidefinite form with real diagonal | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTraceReality.lean` |
| `rpTraceWeight_cauchySchwarz` | **RP infra layer 32** (`RingReflectionCauchySchwarz.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): a reflection-positive trace weight `M` makes `(A,B) ظخ Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖B)` a positive-semidefinite sesquilinear form on the left subalgebra; expanding `0 ظëج Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕(AظêْtB)┬╖(AظêْtB))` as a nonnegative real quadratic in `t` and taking its discriminant (`discrim_le_zero`) gives `(Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖B) + Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕B┬╖A))┬▓ ظëج 4┬╖Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖A)┬╖Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕B┬╖B)` ظ¤ the workhorse for the DLS infrared bound | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionCauchySchwarz.lean` |
| `thermalAverageReS_toHamiltonian_eq` | **RP infra layer 31** (`RingReflectionThermalTransfer.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right-half gauge is a similarity transform, so it leaves the partition function invariant (`thermalPartitionFnS_toHamiltonian_eq`) and transports thermal averages ظ¤ the physical ring Heisenberg canonical average of `A` equals the reflection-positive DLS canonical average of `rightGauge ┬╖ A ┬╖ rightGaugeInv`; the bridge carrying RP bounds back to the physical ring antiferromagnet | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionThermalTransfer.lean` |
| `exp_neg_smul_toHamiltonian_eq` | **RP infra layer 30** (`RingReflectionGibbsGauge.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): conjugation by the right-half gauge commutes with the matrix exponential (`Matrix.exp_units_conj`), so the physical ring Heisenberg Gibbs operator `exp(ظêْ╬▓ ─ج)` and the reflection-positive DLS Gibbs operator `exp(ظêْ╬▓ ┬╖ ringDLSDecomposition.toHamiltonian)` are unitarily equivalent ظ¤ physical thermal averages become DLS thermal averages of gauge-transformed observables | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsGauge.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_ringHamiltonian` | **RP infra layer 29** (`RingReflectionGaugeAssembly.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right-half DLS/Marshall gauge conjugates the ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian into the reflection-positive DLS Hamiltonian ظ¤ `rightGauge ┬╖ heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringCoupling (2n)) ┬╖ rightGaugeInv = ringDLSDecomposition.toHamiltonian` (bond Hamiltonians fixed, the two crossing bonds become the negated gauged crossing interactions); the unitary equivalence bridging the physical ring antiferromagnet to its reflection-positive form | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGaugeAssembly.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_ringLeftHamiltonian` / `_theta_*` | **RP infra layer 28** (`RingReflectionNonCrossConj.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): summing the bond-invariance lemmas, the right-half gauge fixes both the left bond Hamiltonian (`rightGauge_conj_ringLeftHamiltonian`) and the reflected right bond Hamiltonian `╬╕(ringLeftHamiltonian)` (`rightGauge_conj_theta_ringLeftHamiltonian`) ظ¤ only the two crossing bonds are affected | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionNonCrossConj.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_crossBond` | **RP infra layer 27** (`RingReflectionCrossConj.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the gauge image of a crossing bond is the negated gauged crossing interaction. For a left site `x < n` (reflection `r x` a right site), `rightGauge ┬╖ (┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_{r x}) ┬╖ rightGaugeInv = ظêْcrossBondInteractionS x` ظ¤ the left endpoint untouched, the right endpoint gauged (flipping `┼£^1`/`┼£^3` signs), matching `crossBondInteractionS_eq`. Both ring crossing bonds `(nظêْ1,n)` and `(2nظêْ1,0)` are of this form | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionCrossConj.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_spinSDot_left`/`_right` | **RP infra layer 26** (`RingReflectionBondConj.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right-half gauge fixes every non-crossing bond. `rightGauge_conj_spinSDot_left` (left bond, both sites `< n`: untouched) and `rightGauge_conj_spinSDot_right` (right bond, both sites gauged: the `┼£^1`/`┼£^3` sign flips cancel in the product, `┼£^2` unchanged). Only the two crossing bonds acquire the DLS sign flip. Helpers `rightGauge_conj_onSiteS_left`/`_right` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondConj.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_mul` (gauge homomorphism) | **RP infra layer 25** (`RingReflectionGaugeConj.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): conjugation `X ظخ rightGauge┬╖X┬╖rightGaugeInv` by the right-half DLS/Marshall gauge is an algebra homomorphism ظ¤ `rightGauge_conj_mul` (over products, via `rightGaugeInv_mul_rightGauge`), `rightGauge_conj_add`, `rightGauge_conj_smul`, `rightGauge_conj_sum`. | `RPDecomposition.gibbs_rpTraceWeight` (**Gibbs RP capstone**) | **RP infra layer 14** (`RingReflectionGibbsCapstone.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): **full Gibbs reflection positivity** ظ¤ for a reflection-positive decomposition `H = H_L + ╬╕(H_L) ظêْ D` (`RPDecomposition`) and `╬▓ ظëح 0`, the Gibbs weight `exp(ظêْ╬▓┬╖H)` is a reflection-positive trace weight. | `coneRep_exp_add` (kinetic factor) | **RP infra layer 12** (`RingReflectionKineticConeRep.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the kinetic Gibbs factor is cone-representable. | `RPTraceWeightS.mul_coneRep_right` (cone closure) | **RP infra layer 11** (`RingReflectionRPWeightCone.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the reflection-positive trace weights form a convex cone ظ¤ `RPTraceWeightS.zero`/`one` (the identity is RP, the `╬▓=0` trace cone), `add`, `smul_nonneg`, `sum` (closed under finite sums) ظ¤ and `RPTraceWeightS.mul_coneRep_right`: an RP trace weight times a cone-representable operator (`RPTraceConeRepS`) is again RP (since `M┬╖ظêّcط╡ت╬╕(Cط╡ت)Cط╡ت = ظêّcط╡ت(M┬╖╬╕(Cط╡ت)Cط╡ت)`, each RP by the cone-generator closure, summed). This is the algebraic engine that accumulates reflection positivity factor-by-factor in the Trotter expansion of `e^{ظêْ╬▓H}` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRPWeightCone.lean` |
| `RPTraceWeightS.mul_weightGen_right` / `RPTraceWeightS.weightGen_mul_left` | **RP infra layer 10** (`RingReflectionGibbsRP.lean`, Tasaki ┬د4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): closure of reflection-positive trace weights under cone generators `╬╕(L)┬╖L` (`L` left-supported). | `staggeredCasimirOpS` / `shenQiuTian_ferrimagnetic_lro` | **Theorem 4.4** (┬د4.1, ShenظôQiuظôTian, PROVED axiom-free; eqs. (4.1.12)ظô(4.1.13)): ferrimagnetic LRO on an asymmetric bipartite lattice. The SU(2)-invariant squared staggered operator `(├¤_╬ؤ)┬▓ = ╬ث_{x,y} ╬╡_x ╬╡_y ┼£_x┬╖┼£_y`, and the bound `S┬▓(\|A\|ظêْ\|B\|)┬▓ ظëج ظاذ╬خ_GS\|(├¤_╬ؤ)┬▓\|╬خ_GSظار` for any normalized ground state of the connected bipartite AFM model (same hypotheses as Thm 2.3). **PROVED axiom-free** (Issues #4604/#4617 CLOSED; book proof = chain (4.1.16): (4.1.15) cross-term + LiebظôMattis Thm 2.3 total spin + Casimir).
