PR2 (#4606) adds the **cross-term inequality (4.1.15)** in `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROCrossTermCore.lean` (transverse correlation + ladder positivity + per-pair sign) + `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROCrossTerm.lean` (the summed cross-term inequality, split for build speed): the transverse correlation `ظاذ╬خ,T_xy ╬خظار` (`T_xy = ┬╜(┼£_x^+┼£_y^- + ┼£_x^-┼£_y^+)`), its non-positivity for cross-sublattice pairs on a Marshall-positive sector ground state (`marshallSignS┬╖c`, `c>0`), and the summed dominance `ظاذ╬خ,(╬ث_{x,y}T_xy)╬خظار.re ظëج ظاذ╬خ,staggeredTransverseCasimirOpS ╬خظار.re`. PR3 (#4607) adds the **total-spin value** in `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROTotalSpin.lean` (coupling-agnostic via a `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` hypothesis): `exists_centered_groundState_predictedCasimir_of_tasaki23` produces a centered (`┼£┬│_tot ╬خظ░=0`) Marshall ground state with `(┼£_tot)┬▓ ╬خظ░ = S_tot(S_tot+1)┬╖╬خظ░` (`S_tot=(\|A\|ظêْ\|B\|)N/2`), reusing `tasaki23_pf_groundState_casimir_eq_predicted_sector` + the extremal-Casimir machinery. PR4 (#4608) proves the **capstone** `ferrimagnetic_lro_completeBipartite_centered` in `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROCapstone.lean`: assembling chain (4.1.16) on the centered ground state, `(N/2)┬▓(\|A\|ظêْ\|B\|)┬▓┬╖ظاذ╬خظ░,╬خظ░ظار.re ظëج ظاذ╬خظ░,(├¤_╬ؤ)┬▓╬خظ░ظار.re` (existence form, coupling-agnostic via `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3`; sorry-free, axiom-clean, independent of the `shenQiuTian_ferrimagnetic_lro` axiom; that axiom is now itself proved in UniversalFinal). For build speed the proof's private Rayleigh-ratio helpers and the per-sector oriented bound `staggeredCasimir_weightComponent_bound_oriented` (now module-public: `S_tot┬▓┬╖ظû╬خ_Mظû┬▓ ظëج ظاذ╬خ_M,(├¤_╬ؤ)┬▓╬خ_Mظار.re` for each magnetization weight component `╬خ_M` of a ground state) live in the companion `ظخUniversalFinalCore.lean`, with the sum assembly + the public `shenQiuTian_ferrimagnetic_lro` kept in `ظخUniversalFinal.lean`. | `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROUniversalFinal.lean` + `ظخUniversalFinalCore.lean` + `FerrimagneticLRO.lean` + `ظخComponentAlgebra.lean` + `ظخCrossTerm.lean` + `ظخTotalSpin.lean` + `ظخTotalSpinCore.lean` + `ظخCapstone.lean` + `ظخUniversal.lean` |
| `raiseLowerReachableS_of_connected` | **Connected-graph spin-config reachability** (┬د2.5, PROVED; Issue #4609, prereq for the connected MarshallظôLiebظôMattis extension / Thm 4.4 axiom): on a connected graph `G`, any two equal-magnetization configs are raise/lower reachable ظ¤ `G.Connected ظْ magSumS ╧â = magSumS ╧â' ظْ RaiseLowerReachableS G ╧â ╧â'`. Discharged axiom-free by strong induction on the `configDistS` mismatch (surplus/deficit sites + connected-graph walk) with overflow-safe single-quantum path transport (`transportOne`; at each edge push the quantum forward when the next vertex has room, else recurse first to make room). Generalizes the complete-bipartite `raiseLowerReachableS_bipartiteCompleteGraph` to arbitrary connected graphs ظ¤ the combinatorial core of extending Theorem 2.3 to connected couplings (PR #4610). **PR2 (#4611)** lifts this to the dressed-matrix per-sector PerronظôFrobenius positivity for a general connected bipartite `G` in `Quantum/SpinS/ConnectedDressedPF.lean`: `exists_matrixPow_pos_of_magConfigS_connected` (`ظêâ k, 0 < (shiftedDressedSReMatrixOnMagSector A J N c M)^k ╧â' ╧â`) from `G.Connected` + `hGbip` + edge-positivity `hJ_pos_G : G.Adj x y ظْ 0 < (J x y).re` (not complete-bipartite positivity), via the graph-agnostic edge-local witness `neg_dressedHeisenbergSReMatrix_apply_pos_of_raiseLowerStepS_witness` + the connected reachability ظ¤ generalizing `exists_matrixPow_pos_of_magConfigS_bipartite`. **PR3 (#4612)** assembles **Theorem 2.3 itself for connected couplings**: `isIrreducible_shiftedDressedSReMatrixOnMagSector_connected` (ConnectedSectorIrreducible.lean) + 20 `_of_irreducible` chain variants parameterizing the PF-consuming MarshallظôLiebظôMattis chain by the irreducibility result (graph-agnostic) + the capstone `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_data_of_connected` (ConnectedTheorem23Core.lean, ConnectedTheorem23.lean): the per-magnetization-sector Marshall-positive ground states + global minimality of Theorem 2.3 hold for ANY connected bipartite `J` (`G.Connected` + `hGbip` + edge-positivity `hJ_pos_G`), not just complete-bipartite ظ¤ the `hOutside`/lower-bound side reuses `tasaki23_general_hOutside`/`tasaki23_eigenvalue_ge_common` (which need only `hJ_nn`). This is the connected-coupling extension of MarshallظôLiebظôMattis (LiebظôMattis 1962). **PR4 (#4613)** assembles the **connected-coupling ferrimagnetic LRO bound (existence form)** `ferrimagnetic_lro_connected_centered` (ConnectedFerrimagneticLRO.lean): for any connected bipartite AFM coupling (`G.Connected` + `hGbip` + `hJ_pos_G`), there is a centered ground state `╬خظ░` with `(N/2)┬▓(\|A\|ظêْ\|B\|)┬▓┬╖ظاذ╬خظ░,╬خظ░ظار.re ظëج ظاذ╬خظ░,(├¤_╬ؤ)┬▓╬خظ░ظار.re` ظ¤ Tasaki's chain (4.1.16) for the genuine connected (non-complete) coupling, built from the connected Theorem 2.3 data + connected irreducibility Casimir value + the PR1/PR2 operator-algebra (all graph-agnostic). **PR5 (#4614)** proves the **`SU(2)` invariance of `(├¤_╬ؤ)┬▓`** in `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredCasimirSU2Invariance.lean`: `staggeredCasimirOpS_commute_totalSpinSOp{1,2,3}` + `ظخOpPlus`/`ظخOpMinus` (the squared staggered order operator commutes with every total-spin operator), immediate from the per-pair `spinSDot_commutator_totalSpinSOp*` vanishing ظ¤ ingredient (a) of the universal-form transfer. **PR6 (#4615)** proves the **core of ingredient (b)** in `Quantum/SpinS/SU2ExpectationLadderInvariant.lean`: `su2_expectationRatioRe_ladder_invariant` ظ¤ for an SU(2)-invariant operator `O` (commuting with `┼£^┬▒_tot`) and a joint `┼£┬│_tot`/Casimir eigenvector `v`, the real expectation ratio `ظاذw,Owظار.re/ظاذw,wظار.re` is unchanged under lowering `w ظخ ┼£ظ╗_tot v` (both numerator and denominator scale by the same `┼£ظ║┼£ظ╗ = Casimir ظêْ (┼£┬│)┬▓ + ┼£┬│` eigenvalue). This is the ladder step that makes `(├¤_╬ؤ)┬▓`'s expectation constant across the spin-`S_tot` multiplet; **PR7 (#4616)** iterates it to `(┼£ظ╗_tot)^k` (`su2_expectationRatioRe_ladder_iterate_invariant`, `Quantum/SpinS/SU2ExpectationLadderIterated.lean`), so every member of the lowering tower shares the centered ratio. The **stated** `shenQiuTian_ferrimagnetic_lro` axiom (universal over *all* ground states) now remains pending only the final assembly of ingredient (b): the ground-state weight decomposition + the **ground-eigenspace classification** (any global-min eigenvector's nonzero `┼£┬│`-weight components lie in the admissible sectors ظ¤ needs strict energy separation, the hard remaining gate) (#4604). **PR8 (#4618)** adds step 1 of that dimension bound: `heisenbergHamiltonianSMatrixOnMagSector_finrank_le_one_of_marshall_positive_connected` (`Quantum/SpinS/ConnectedSectorFinrankLeOne.lean`) ظ¤ connected-coupling per-sector ground-state uniqueness (`finrank ظëج 1`), the connected-irreducibility mirror of the complete-graph lemma (Issue #4617). **PR9 (#4619)** adds step 3: `weightPreserving_expectation_eq_sum_sector` (`Quantum/SpinS/WeightPreservingExpectationSum.lean`) ظ¤ for any operator commuting with `┼£┬│_tot`, `ظاذ╬خ,O╬خظار = ╬ث_M ظاذ╬خ_M,O╬خ_Mظار` over the `┼£┬│`-weight sector components (cross terms vanish by `┼£┬│_tot`-eigenvalue orthogonality; no Hermiticity of `O` needed), the block-diagonal decomposition for `(├¤_╬ؤ)┬▓`'s expectation. **PR10 (#4620)** clears the hard gate (step 2): `tasaki23_strict_hOutside_of_connected` (`Quantum/SpinS/StrictHOutsideFerrimagnetic.lean`; for build speed the per-sector ferrimagnetic foundation `tasaki23_strict_hOutside_ferrimagnetic` and its Casimir-obstruction helpers live in the companion `ظخStrictHOutsideFerrimagneticCore.lean`) ظ¤ **strict** energy separation, `╬╝ < ╬╝M` for any non-admissible sector `M ظêë [min┬╖N, max┬╖N]` (the connected ferrimagnetic generalization of the balanced `tasaki23_strict_hOutside_of_card_eq_zero_casimir_ladder_obstruction`, via the Casimir equality-obstruction: ladder a hypothetical non-admissible ╬╝-eigenvector to the admissible band edge, pull back the predicted Casimir `S_tot(S_tot+1)` through the ladder, contradict the abs Casimir lower bound `|centerظêْM|(|centerظêْM|+1) > S_tot(S_tot+1)`). This is the strict LiebظôMattis level ordering for the connected AFM Heisenberg ظ¤ the last gate; only the final weight-decomposition assembly remains to discharge `shenQiuTian_ferrimagnetic_lro` (#4617). **PR11 (#4621)** adds the universal-form assembly infra in `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROUniversal.lean`: `chain_bound_marshall_sector` (Tasaki's chain (4.1.16) at an arbitrary-weight Marshall sector vector: `(╬│ ظêْ m┬▓)ظûwظû┬▓ ظëج ظاذw,(├¤_╬ؤ)┬▓wظار.re`), `star_dotProduct_self_eq_sum_sector` (`ظû╬خظû┬▓ = ╬ث_M ظû╬خ_Mظû┬▓`), `heisenbergHamiltonianS_magSectorProjection_eigen` (a weight component of an H-eigenvector is an H-eigenvector), the diagonal-shift `c` existence, and the global-flip / weight-commute helpers. The remaining step to remove the axiom is the SU(2) Rayleigh-ratio constancy across the spin-`S_tot` multiplet (transfer the per-sector bound from a near-central admissible sector to all sectors via the merged iterated ladder-invariance) (#4617) | `Quantum/SpinS/ConnectedRaiseLower.lean` + `ظخConnectedDressedPF.lean` + `ظخConnectedSectorIrreducible.lean` + `ظخConnectedTheorem23Core.lean` + `ظخConnectedTheorem23.lean` + `ظخConnectedFerrimagneticLRO.lean` + `ظخStaggeredCasimirSU2Invariance.lean` + `ظخSU2ExpectationLadderInvariant.lean` + `ظخSU2ExpectationLadderIterated.lean` + `ظخConnectedSectorFinrankLeOne.lean` + `ظخWeightPreservingExpectationSum.lean` + `ظخStrictHOutsideFerrimagnetic.lean` + `ظخStrictHOutsideFerrimagneticCore.lean` + `ظخFerrimagneticLROUniversal.lean` |

**spectral completeness/finiteness** (`hhafRealSpectrum_restrict` reverse bridge, `hhafRealSpectrum_subset_eigenvalue_range` via mathlib `spectrum_real_eq_range_eigenvalues`, `hhafRealSpectrum_finite`) ظ¤ the restricted spectrum IS the finite restricted-matrix eigenvalue set. **Diagonal bounds**: new general `HermitianVariationalUpperBound` (max-Rayleigh) + `hhafMinEnergy_le_diag`/`diag_le_hhafMaxEnergy` give `hhafMinEnergy ظëج (restMat ╧â ╧â).re ظëج hhafMaxEnergy` via Rayleigh at the basis vector (toward non-scalarity for the gap). **Non-scalarity PROVED**: `hhafMinEnergy_lt_hhafMaxEnergy` (even Lظëح2) via all-zero config (diag=0) and domain-wall config ╧â=[0,2,1,ظخ,1] (HAF, diag<0); general formula `hhaf_diag_eq_succ_sum` (diag = ╬ث_x (1-╧âx)(1-╧â_{x+1})). **Positive gap PROVED**: `exists_hhaf_positive_gap` (even L>0) ظ¤ first-excited eigenvalue Eظé = (eigenvalues image filtered above min).min with Eظé ظêê spectrum, Eظé > minEig (gap>0), and minimal among spectrum elements above minEig (via completeness). These foundations feed the now-discharged uniqueness clause. **PF real-form foundation** started: `hhafRestrictedMatrix_im_zero` (entries real), `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal` (real symmetric form) + `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal_ofReal`/`_isSymm` ظ¤ the entry point for the Perron-Frobenius uniqueness route (Marshall gauge (now built: `ringSublattice`, `hhafDressedMatrix`, `hhafDressedMatrix_eq` = ╬╡(╧â)╬╡(╧)┬╖M_real via the dressed-Heisenberg machinery) + **symmetrized-coupling infra** for the off-diagonal sign step (`heisenbergHamiltonianS_coupling_swap`: the Heisenberg Hamiltonian is invariant under transposing the coupling, via `spinSDot_comm`; `ringCouplingSym` = `ringCoupling + ringCouplingط╡` with `_symm`/`_im_zero`/`_re_nonneg`; `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCouplingSym`: `H(ringCouplingSym) = 2┬╖H(ringCoupling)`, so the directed ring Hamiltonian equals ┬╜ the symmetric-coupling one ظ¤ letting the symmetric-coupling off-diagonal nonpositivity lemma apply) ظ¤ **off-diagonal nonpositivity now PROVED**: `ringCouplingSym_bipartite` (even ring `L` is bipartite w.r.t. `ringSublattice`: for even `L`, `(a+1)%L` flips parity, so equal-parity sites are never adjacent ظ¤ via `Nat.mod_mod_of_dvd`), `dressedHeisenbergS_ringCoupling_re_eq_half` (dressed directed = ┬╜ dressed symmetric), and `hhafDressedMatrix_offdiag_nonpos` (even `L`: `hhafDressedMatrix ╧â ╧ ظëج 0` for `╧â ظëب ╧`, via the symmetric-coupling nonpositivity lemma) ظ¤ the diagonal `┬▒1` Marshall gauge makes the AFM off-diagonals sign-definite. **Symmetry + nonneg shift now PROVED**: `hhafDressedMatrix_isSymm` (the dressed matrix is symmetric), `hhafDressedMatrix_diag_eq` (diagonal = real restricted diagonal, since the Marshall sign squares to `1`), `hhafDressedMatrix_diag_le_hhafMaxEnergy` (diagonal ظëج max energy), and `hhafShifted_entry_nonneg` (for `c ظëح hhafMaxEnergy` and even `L`, the shifted matrix `c┬╖I ظêْ M` has nonnegative entries ظ¤ the nonnegativity hypothesis of `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric`). **Irreducibility started** (Issue #4732, G├│mez-Santos kink ergodicity): `hhafShifted_pos_of_ladderStep` ظ¤ two hidden-AFM configs differing by one adjacent raise/lower (ladder) move on a bipartite bond have a strictly positive shifted entry `(c┬╖I ظêْ M) ╧ ╧â > 0` (reusing `dressedHeisenbergS_apply_re_neg_of_raiseLowerStepS_witness` on `ringCouplingSym` + the ┬╜ scaling), the single-edge positivity for the PerronظôFrobenius reachability argument. **Ring bond graph** added: `hhafRingGraph` (cyclic nearest-neighbour graph on `Fin L`), with `hhafRingGraph_adj_sublattice_ne` (bipartite w.r.t. `ringSublattice` for even `L`) and `ringCouplingSym_re_pos_of_ringGraph_adj` (the symmetrized coupling is strictly positive on every edge) ظ¤ the bond-graph data feeding the per-step positivity. **HAF reachability + walkظْpower** added: `RaiseLowerStepSHhaf` / `RaiseLowerReachableSHhaf` (single HAF-preserving ladder move / its reflexive-transitive closure), `hhafShifted_pos_of_stepHhaf` (each step gives a strictly positive shifted entry), and `exists_matrixPow_apply_pos_of_hhafReachable` (HAF-reachability ظçْ some power `(c┬╖I ظêْ M)^k` is strictly positive ظ¤ the walkظْpower bridge, mirroring the magnetization-sector version). **Kink-reduction setup** added: `hhafCanonical` (the all-`0`-spin configuration, base case), `pmCount` (number of `┬▒` spins, the induction measure), `pmCount_eq_zero_iff` (no `┬▒` spins ظا║ canonical). **Slide move** added: `slidePM` (move the spin at `a` onto a cyclic neighbour `b`, leaving a `0` at `a`), with `slidePM_apply` (its pointwise values) and `slidePM_isRaiseLowerStep` (it is a single raise/lower ladder step on the ring-graph bond `{a, b}` when `a` carries a `┬▒` spin and `b` carries a `0`). **Slide preserves hidden-AFM order PROVEN** (the kink-ergodicity core): `slidePM_isHiddenAFM` ظ¤ sliding a `┬▒` spin onto its `0`-carrying cyclic successor keeps the strict `+,ظêْ,ظخ` alternation, proved by transferring each `IsNextPM` pair of the moved configuration back to one of `╧â` (relabelling `b ظخ a`) via the open-arc decomposition lemmas `inCyclicOpen_succ_left_imp` / `inCyclicOpen_succ_right_imp` / `notInCyclicOpen_succ` (each an `omega` calculation after splitting the cyclic wrap). **Slide lifted to a subtype step** (`slidePM_isRaiseLowerStepSHhaf`) and the **annihilation move** added: `annihPM` (set adjacent `a, b` both to `0`), `annihPM_apply`, and `annihPM_isRaiseLowerStep` (it is a single ladder step when `a, b` carry opposite `┬▒` spins). **Annihilation preserves hidden-AFM order PROVEN**: `annihPM_isHiddenAFM` ظ¤ removing an adjacent opposite-sign `+,ظêْ` pair keeps the alternation (each `IsNextPM` pair of the annihilated config either avoids the removed pair, giving an `IsNextPM` pair of `╧â`, or straddles it, where the chain `x, a, b, y` forces opposite endpoint signs), via the sub-arc lemmas `inCyclicOpen_succ_iff_mem` / `inCyclicOpen_sub_left` / `inCyclicOpen_sub_right` / `notInCyclicOpen_succ_right` / `notInCyclicOpen_pred_left`. **Move-count behaviour** added: `annihPM_isRaiseLowerStepSHhaf` (annihilation as a subtype step), `slidePM_pmCount_card` (sliding preserves the `┬▒` count), `annihPM_pmCount_card_lt` (annihilation strictly decreases it). **CONNECTIVITY PROVEN** (the kink-ergodicity theorem): the reduction measure `hhafS = ╬ث_{┬▒ spins}(L ظêْ x)` strictly decreases under a slide (`slidePM_hhafS_lt`, non-wrapping successor) and an annihilation (`annihPM_hhafS_lt`); `hhaf_single_step` produces, for any balanced (even-`pmCount`) non-canonical config, a single HAF ladder move to another balanced config of smaller measure (first `┬▒` spin at `p ظëج Lظêْ2`, sliding past a `0` or annihilating an adjacent opposite `┬▒`); `hhaf_reachable_to_canonical` / `hhaf_reachable_canonical` (strong induction on `hhafS`) then show **every balanced hidden-AFM configuration is HAF-reachable from the canonical all-`0` configuration** (with `RaiseLowerReachableSHhaf_symm` for the reverse). **Design note**: the *full* HAF matrix is reducible ظ¤ a single-`┬▒` config (`pmCount = 1`, magnetization `┬▒1`) is vacuously hidden-AFM but unreachable from `canonical` (the moves conserve magnetization), so irreducibility holds only within the balanced (charge-`0`) sector; this is the sector containing the antiferromagnetic ground state. **Balanced-sector block irreducibility PROVEN**: `hhafConfig0` (the even-`pmCount` subtype) with `hhafShiftedMatrix0` (the `c┬╖I ظêْ M` block), `hhaf_reachable_to_canonical0` / `RaiseLowerReachableSHhaf0_symm` (balanced-sector connectivity lifted to the subtype), `exists_matrixPow_apply_pos_of_hhafReachable0` (subtype walkظْpower), and `hhafShiftedMatrix0_isIrreducible` ظ¤ for even `L` and shift `c > hhafMaxEnergy`, the balanced-sector shifted matrix is PerronظôFrobenius irreducible (`isIrreducible_iff_exists_pow_pos`: nonnegative + entrywise-positive power, with the diagonal handled by the strict shift `c > hhafMaxEnergy`). **Balanced-sector unique ground state PROVEN**: `hhafDressedMatrix0` (the dressed block on `hhafConfig0`), `hhafDressedMatrix0_isSymm` (submatrix of a symmetric matrix), `hhafShiftedMatrix0_eq` (`hhafShiftedMatrix0 = c┬╖I ظêْ Mظé`, the identity restricting since `Subtype.val` is injective), and `hhafDressedMatrix0_ground_finrank_le_one` ظ¤ applying `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric` to the balanced block yields a strictly positive lowest eigenvector with a **one-dimensional** ground eigenspace: the unique ground state of the AFM chain *within the balanced (charge-`0`) sector* of `H_HAF`. **Marshall-gauge transfer DONE**: `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal0` (undressed real balanced block), `hhafMarshallDiag0` (the `┬▒1` Marshall sign diagonal, squaring to `1`), `hhafDressedMatrix0_eq_conj` (`Mظé = ╬ء┬╖M_realظé┬╖╬ء`), and `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal0_ground_finrank_le_one` ظ¤ transferring the **full PerronظôFrobenius data** (nonzero lowest eigenvector `╬ءv`, ground minimality, and `finrank ظëج 1`) through the Marshall similarity (the generic `matrix_similar_eigenspace_finrank_eq`, now generalised from `ظé` to any field), so the **undressed** real balanced block also has a one-dimensional ground eigenspace with an explicit minimal eigenvector. Remaining for the *global* ground-state uniqueness: the inter-sector energy ordering (balanced sector strictly lowest, a MarshallظôLiebظôMattis statement within `H_HAF`). **N├رel witness** added (toward that ordering): `hhafNeel` (the maximally-AFM configuration `+1` on even sites, `ظêْ1` on odd), `hhafNeel_isHiddenAFM` (it is hidden-AFM on an even ring ظ¤ all sites are `┬▒`, every `IsNextPM` pair is cyclically adjacent hence opposite parity, via `inCyclicOpen_succ_mem_of_ne`), `hhafNeel_pmCount` (`┬▒`-count `= L`), and `hhafNeelConfig0` (the N├رel configuration as a balanced charge-`0` configuration). **Ground energy ظëج ظêْL PROVEN**: `hhafNeel_diag` (the N├رel diagonal energy is `ظêْL`, every nearest-neighbour bond antiferromagnetic `(+1)(ظêْ1) = ظêْ1`) and `hhafMinEnergy_le_neg_L` (the global restricted ground energy `hhafMinEnergy ظëج ظêْL`, via the Rayleigh quotient `hhafMinEnergy_le_diag` at the N├رel state) ظ¤ the first half of the inter-sector ordering (the balanced sector reaches energy `ظêْL`; the remaining half bounds the magnetization-`┬▒1` single-`┬▒` sectors above this). Toward that bound: `hhaf_diag_eq_zero_of_pmCount_one` ظ¤ a single-`┬▒` configuration has **vanishing diagonal energy** (every nearest-neighbour bond has a `0`-spin endpoint since only one site is `┬▒`), the Gershgorin-disc centre for the magnetization-`┬▒1` sectors. `spinSDot_re_abs_le_one_raising_lowering` / `spinSDot_re_abs_le_one_lowering_raising` ظ¤ the spin-`1` ladder off-diagonal amplitude `|ظاذ╧â'|┼£_x┬╖┼£_y|╧âظار| ظëج 1` (each `ظêأ` factor `ظëج ظêأ2` for `N = 2`), the Gershgorin-disc radius ingredient. `hhafDressedMatrix_abs_le_ringCouplingSym` ظ¤ for two hidden-AFM configs related by a single ladder move on a bond `{x, y}`, the dressed off-diagonal entry satisfies `|M ╧â ╧| ظëج (ringCouplingSym x y).re` (the Marshall signs are `┬▒1`, the Heisenberg element is `ringCouplingSym ┬╖ spinSDot`, and the ladder amplitude is `ظëج 1`). **Magnetization-`┬▒1` sector min `ظëح ظêْ2` PROVEN** (codex insight: the cyclic `IsHiddenAFMConfig` forbids odd `pmCount > 1`, so the only HAF sectors are balanced `Sz=0` (even `pmCount`) and single-`┬▒` `Sz=┬▒1` (`pmCount = 1`) ظ¤ there are **no higher-`Sz` sectors**, dissolving the inter-sector LiebظôMattis wall): `hhafConfigPM1` (the `pmCount = 1` subtype) with `hhafDressedMatrixPM1` (dressed block), `hhafPM1Site` (the unique `┬▒` site, computed via `Finset.min'` so `Finset.image` reductions stay light), `hhafPM1_coupling_imp` (a nonzero off-diagonal forces `╧` to be the slide `slidePM` of `╧â`'s `┬▒` spin to its new site, sharing the `┬▒` value by magnetization conservation `magEigenvalueS_eq_iff`), `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_offdiag_le` (per-entry bound `ظëج ringCouplingSym`), `hhafPM1Site_injOn_support` (the `┬▒`-site map is injective on the coupling support), `ringCouplingSym_re_row_sum` (`ظêّ_t (ringCouplingSym s t).re = 2`, the two incident directed ring bonds), the generic `sum_comp_le_sum_univ_of_injOn` (injective-on-support reindex avoiding a heavy concrete `Finset.univ`), and `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_rowSum_le_two` ظا╣ `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_eigenvalue_ge` (Gershgorin `eigenvalue_mem_ball`, diagonal `0` + row sum `ظëج 2`: every single-`┬▒` eigenvalue `ظëح ظêْ2`) ظ¤ the single-kink tight-binding lower bound placing the `Sz = ┬▒1` sectors above the balanced ground energy (`ظëج ظêْL ظëج ظêْ2`). **Balanced ground energy `< ظêْL` STRICT PROVEN** (`hhafDressedMatrix0_ground_lt_neg_L`): the PerronظôFrobenius lowest eigenvector `v > 0` paired with the N├رel row gives `╬╝┬╖v(N├رel) = ظêْL┬╖v(N├رel) + ╬ث_{╧ظëبN├رel} Mظé(N├رel,╧) v(╧)`; every off-diagonal term is `ظëج 0` (`hhafDressedMatrix0_offdiag_nonpos`) and the annihilation neighbour (`hhafNeel_step_neighbor`, annihilating the adjacent opposite-`┬▒` pair at sites `0,1`) is strictly `< 0`, so via the generic row inequality `row_sum_mul_lt_diag_mul_of_offdiag_nonpos_exists_neg` and `hhafDressedMatrix0_Neel_diag` (`Mظé(N├رel,N├رel) = ظêْL`) we get `╬╝┬╖v(N├رel) < ظêْL┬╖v(N├رel)`, hence `╬╝ < ظêْL`. Since `ظêْL ظëج ظêْ2`, the balanced sector lies **strictly** below the magnetization-`┬▒1` sectors with **no `L=2` tie** ظ¤ the inter-sector ordering is complete. **Sector classification PROVEN** (`hhaf_pmCount_eq_one_or_even`): every hidden-AFM configuration has `pmCount = 1` or even `pmCount` ظ¤ an odd count `ظëح 3` is impossible. Proof: the `┬▒` sites read in increasing index order are pairwise `IsNextPM` (`hhaf_isNextPM_consecutive` ظ¤ no `┬▒` spin lies strictly between index-adjacent `┬▒` sites, via `orderEmbOfFin`), so their signs strictly alternate (`hhaf_pm_alternates`: the `i`-th `┬▒` value is the `0`-th when `i` is even and its complement `2 - ┬╖` when odd, `pm_flip`); and the maximal/minimal `┬▒` sites are also `IsNextPM` (`hhaf_isNextPM_wrap` ظ¤ the cyclic wrap arc holds no `┬▒` spin), which would force the first and last `┬▒` signs to *differ* while the linear alternation makes them *equal* at odd count ظ¤ contradiction. The magnetization corollaries `hhaf_magSumS_eq_L_of_even` (even `pmCount ظا╣ magSumS = L`, via `magSumS`-invariance under HAF ladder moves `magSumS_eq_of_hhafReachable` + reachability from canonical) and `hhaf_magSumS_ne_L_of_pmCount_one` (single-`┬▒` ظا╣ `magSumS = L ┬▒ 1 ظëب L`) identify the sectors: balanced `= Sz 0`, single-`┬▒` `= Sz ┬▒1`, with no higher sectors ظ¤ the cross-sector vanishing (magnetization conservation) needed to split a ground eigenvector into balanced + single-`┬▒` blocks. **Magnetization-block structure of the restricted matrix PROVEN**: `hhafRestrictedMatrix_eq_zero_of_magSumS_ne` (entries between different-`magSumS` configurations vanish ظ¤ magnetization conservation); the generic slice `hhafRestrictedMatrixSlice P` (submatrix on `{╧â // P ╧â}`) with `hhafRestrictedMatrixSlice_mulVec_of_full_eigen` (a full eigenvector restricted to a block-closed `P`-slice is a `P`-slice eigenvector with the same eigenvalue); and the two block-closures `hhafRestrictedMatrix_pmCount_one_block_closed` / `hhafRestrictedMatrix_even_block_closed` (via the classification: a single-`┬▒` config couples only to single-`┬▒`, an even config only to even) ظ¤ the block-diagonal decomposition into the balanced and single-`┬▒` blocks, the structural input for the ground-eigenspace `finrank ظëج 1`. **Per-block spectral bounds PROVEN**: the complex blocks are the real blocks cast to `ظé` (`hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrix0_eq_map` / `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrixPM1_eq_map`, entries real); `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrix0_eigenspace_finrank_le_one` ظ¤ the **complex balanced block ground eigenspace is one-dimensional** (realظْcomplex bridge `matrix_complex_eigenspace_finrank_le_one_of_real` from the real balanced PerronظôFrobenius `finrank ظëج 1`); and `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrixPM1_eigenvalue_ge` ظ¤ the **complex single-`┬▒` block has all eigenvalues `ظëح ظêْ2`** (an eigenvalue is real, its re/im part is a real-block eigenvector, transferred to a dressed-block eigenvector via the Marshall conjugation `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_eq_conj` where the Gershgorin bound `ظëح ظêْ2` applies). These are the per-block spectral inputs: at the ground energy `E < ظêْ2` the single-`┬▒` block is vacated and the balanced block contributes the unique ground state. **Reverse block restriction PROVEN**: `hhafSliceExtend P` (zero-extension of a `P`-slice vector to all of `hhafConfig`) with `hhafSliceExtend_ne_zero` and `hhafRestrictedMatrix_mulVec_hhafSliceExtend` (if there is no coupling *into* a `P`-slice from its complement, the zero-extension of a `P`-slice eigenvector is a full eigenvector at the same eigenvalue) ظ¤ the reverse counterpart of the forward slice restriction, used to lift the balanced PerronظôFrobenius eigenvector to a full restricted-matrix eigenvector (so the balanced ground energy is in the restricted spectrum). (The PerronظôFrobenius uniqueness development ظ¤ kink moves through the Marshall transfer, the magnetization-`┬▒1` Gershgorin bound, and the strict balanced ground energy ظ¤ lives in the companion `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniqueness.lean`, with its combinatorial kink-move/balanced-sector core split off into `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniquenessCore.lean` for build speed.) | `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrder.lean` + `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniqueness.lean` + `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniquenessCore.lean` |

(7.1.1)ظô(7.1.2)): the AffleckظôKennedyظôLiebظôTasaki `S=1` chain. `akltHamiltonianS L = ╬ث_x {┼£_x┬╖┼£_{x+1} + ظàô(┼£_x┬╖┼£_{x+1})┬▓}` (eq. 7.1.1, PBC ring, SU(2)-invariant; the biquadratic term puts it outside MarshallظôLiebظôMattis). For range `r` bounded by `vظé`, ظêâ `╬╡ظé>0` such that for `|╬╡|<╬╡ظé` there are `╬¤E,C,╬╛>0` **L-independent** with: for any `L ظëح 3` and any such `v`, the perturbed AKLT chain has a **unique ground state** (`IsUniqueChainGroundState`), a **gap ظëح ╬¤E** (`IsPositiveSpectralGap`), and **exponential decay of the connected correlation** `|connectedChainCorrelation| ظëج C e^{ظêْd(x,y)/╬╛}` (`ringDist`). Pigeonhole/cardinality argument: the `(0, 1)`-disagreement and `(1, 0)`-disagreement sets have equal cardinality from magnetisation equality, and the disagreement set is non-empty for `╧â ظëب ╧â'` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |

Combined with `J(x, y).re > 0` on graph edges gives strict negativity of M off-diagonal at swap pairs, hence strict positivity of `B = c┬╖I ظêْ M` ظ¤ the input for PerronظôFrobenius irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/DressedSwapValue.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_pos_of_basisSwap` | strict positivity `0 < B ╧â ╧` on swap-related H_0 configurations with positive symmetric bipartite `J`. Combines the dressed matrix value `-J(x, y).re` (PR ╬▒-5i) with the off-diagonal definition `B ╧â ╧ = -M ╧â ╧` (PR ╬▒-5d). Single-step strict positivity for PerronظôFrobenius irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0ShiftedSwap.lean` |
| `matrix_pow_succ_pos_of_path` | generic matrix-power positivity from a positive path: for non-negative matrix `B` and a path `p_0, ..., p_{k+1}` with `B(p_i, p_{i+1}) > 0` on every consecutive pair, `(B^(k+1))(p_0)(p_{k+1}) > 0`. Induction on `k` using `pow_succ` + `Finset.sum_pos'`. Used to lift single-step swap positivity (╬▒-5j) to multi-step matrix-power positivity for PF irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MatrixPowPath.lean` |
| `matrix_pow_succ_pos_of_pow_pos_step` | one-step extension: if `(B^m) ╧â ╧ > 0` and `B ╧ ╧ > 0` for non-negative `B`, then `(B^(m+1)) ╧â ╧ > 0`. Inductive building block for ReflTransGen-style matrix-power lifting | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MatrixPowExtend.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_pow_pos_of_swapReachable` | for `╧â : HظéIndex ╬ؤ` and any `╬╛` with `Relation.ReflTransGen (SwapStep G) ╧â.val ╬╛`, there exists `m` with `(B^m) ╧â ظاذ╬╛, h_magظار > 0`. Induction on `ReflTransGen`: refl gives `m = 0`, tail extends by one swap using ╬▒-5j (single-step swap positivity) and ╬▒-5l (one-step matrix-power extension). Key bridge from combinatorial reachability to PF irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0ShiftedReachable.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isIrreducible` | **`B = c ┬╖ I ظêْ M` is irreducible** on `H_0` for connected bipartite `G` with positive symmetric real coupling supported on `G`-edges and shift constant `c > M ╧â ╧â` (strict). Cases on `╧â = ╧` (use diagonal positivity) vs `╧â ظëب ╧` (use ╬▒-5c reachability + ╬▒-5m matrix-power lift). | `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isHermitian` | the shifted matrix is Hermitian (= symmetric for real matrices). Wraps `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isSymm` (PR ╬▒-5d) into the IsHermitian form needed by PerronظôFrobenius | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0PFApplication.lean` |

From the bilinear expansion (PR #1058) plus diagonal `┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_x = N(N+2)/4 ┬╖ 1` and off-diagonal `┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y ┬╖ \|╧â_ظèجظار = N┬▓/4 ┬╖ \|╧â_ظèجظار` for `x ظëب y`. | `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | **spin-`S` toy Hamiltonian eigenvalue on the all-up state**: `─ج_toy_S ┬╖ \|╧â_ظèجظار = ((\|╬ؤ\|┬╖N/2)(\|╬ؤ\|┬╖N/2+1) ظêْ (\|A\|┬╖N/2)(\|A\|┬╖N/2+1) ظêْ (\|┬شA\|┬╖N/2)(\|┬شA\|┬╖N/2+1)) ┬╖ \|╧â_ظèجظار`. Direct combination of closed form (PR #1056), total Casimir on `\|╧â_ظèجظار` (existing in `AllAlignedStateCore.lean`), and sublattice Casimirs on `\|╧â_ظèجظار` (PR #1059) (╬│-4 step 19) | `Quantum/SpinS/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` (PR #1060) |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero_simplified` | **simplified spin-`S` toy Hamiltonian eigenvalue on the all-up state**: `─ج_toy_S ┬╖ \|╧â_ظèجظار = (\|A\|┬╖\|┬شA\|┬╖N┬▓/2) ┬╖ \|╧â_ظèجظار`. Direct corollary of steps 205/206 (spin-`S`) and 204/207 (spin-`1/2`); witnesses that every basis state is a sharp `┼£_tot^(3)`-eigenstate (╬│-4 step 208) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateExpectations.lean` and `Quantum/SpinDot/HamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1255) |
| `basisVecS_off_diagonal_totalSpinSOp3` and spin-1/2 mirror `basisVec_off_diagonal_totalSpinHalfOp3` | **Off-diagonal `┼£_tot^(3)` matrix elements vanish**: `<basisVecS ╧ \| ┼£_tot^(3) \| basisVecS ╧â> = 0` for `╧ ظëب ╧â` (spin-`S`), and analogue for spin-`1/2`. Witnesses that `┼£_tot^(3)` is diagonal in the computational basis (╬│-4 step 209) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateExpectations.lean` and `Quantum/SpinDot/HamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1256) |
| `basisVecS_dotProduct_basisVecS_of_ne` and spin-1/2 mirror `basisVec_dotProduct_basisVec_of_ne` | **Basis orthogonality**: `<basisVecS ╧ \| basisVecS ╧â> = 0` for `╧ ظëب ╧â` (spin-`S`), and analogue for spin-`1/2`. Companion to the existing diagonal `_inner_self` (= 1) lemma; together they witness that the computational basis is orthonormal (╬│-4 step 210) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateExpectations.lean` and `Quantum/SpinDot/HamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1257) |
| `basisVecS_dotProduct_basisVecS` and spin-1/2 mirror `basisVec_dotProduct_basisVec` | **Combined ╬┤-form**: `<basisVecS ╧ \| basisVecS ╧â> = if ╧ = ╧â then 1 else 0` (and spin-`1/2` analogue). Single-statement combination of the diagonal-1 and off-diagonal-0 facts (╬│-4 step 211) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateExpectations.lean` and `Quantum/SpinDot/HamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1258) |
| `basisVecS_dotProduct_totalSpinSOp3_basisVecS` and spin-1/2 mirror `basisVec_dotProduct_totalSpinHalfOp3_basisVec` | **Combined `┼£_tot^(3)` ╬┤-form matrix element**: `<basisVecS ╧ \| ┼£_tot^(3) \| basisVecS ╧â> = if ╧ = ╧â then magEigenvalueS ╧â else 0` (spin-`S`); spin-`1/2` analogue with `M(╧â)/2`. Single-statement combination of ╬│-4 step 206/207 (diagonal) and ╬│-4 step 209 (off-diagonal) (╬│-4 step 212) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateExpectations.lean` and `Quantum/SpinDot/HamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1259) |
| `basisVecS_dotProduct_totalSpinSOp3_sq_basisVecS` and spin-1/2 mirror `basisVec_dotProduct_totalSpinHalfOp3_sq_basisVec` | **Combined `(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓` ╬┤-form matrix element**: `<basisVecS ╧ \| (┼£_tot^(3))┬▓ \| basisVecS ╧â> = if ╧ = ╧â then (magEigenvalueS ╧â)┬▓ else 0` (spin-`S`); spin-`1/2` analogue with `(M(╧â)/2)┬▓`. Off-diagonal proven via the `┼£_tot^(3)` eigenvector identity applied twice + basis orthogonality (╬│-4 step 213) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateExpectations.lean` and `Quantum/SpinDot/HamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1260) |
| `basisVecS_expectation_totalSpinSOp1` / `_totalSpinSOp2` | **Spin-`S` transverse `┼£_tot^(1,2)` zero expectation on every basis state**: `<basisVecS ╧â \| ┼£_tot^(1) \| basisVecS ╧â> = 0` and `<basisVecS ╧â \| ┼£_tot^(2) \| basisVecS ╧â> = 0`. Direct from per-site `basisVecS_expectation_onSiteS_spinSOp{1,2}` summed over sites via `Matrix.sum_mulVec` + `dotProduct_sum` (╬│-4 step 214) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleSiteTransverseMeanZero.lean` (PR #1261) |
| `basisVec_expectation_totalSpinHalfOp1` / `_totalSpinHalfOp2` | **Spin-`1/2` transverse `┼£_tot^(1,2)` zero expectation on every basis state**: `<basisVec ╧â \| ┼£_tot^(1) \| basisVec ╧â> = 0` and `<basisVec ╧â \| ┼£_tot^(2) \| basisVec ╧â> = 0`. | `basisVec_totalSpinHalfSquared_expectation_general` | **Spin-`1/2` `(┼£_tot)┬▓` Casimir expectation on arbitrary `basisVec ╧â`**: `<basisVec ╧â \| (┼£_tot)┬▓ \| basisVec ╧â> = \|╬ؤ\|/2 + (M(╧â)/2)┬▓` for any `╧â : ╬ؤ ظْ Fin 2`. Generalises ╬│-4 step 201 (constant case has `M = ┬▒\|╬ؤ\|` giving `\|╬ؤ\|┬╖(\|╬ؤ\|+2)/4`). Proof: reduce to diagonal matrix element, expand `(┼£_tot)┬▓ = ╬ث_{x,y} spinHalfDot x y`, unify per-pair diagonal as `m_x m_y/4 + (1/2)┬╖[x=y]`, then split via separability (╬│-4 step 216) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1263) |
| `basisVecS_totalSpinSSquared_expectation_general` | **Spin-`S` `(┼£_tot)┬▓` Casimir expectation on arbitrary `basisVecS ╧â`**: `<basisVecS ╧â \| (┼£_tot)┬▓ \| basisVecS ╧â> = \|V\|┬╖N(N+2)/4 + magEigenvalueS(╧â)┬▓ ظêْ ╬ث_x (N/2 ظêْ ╧âx.val)┬▓` for any `╧â : ╬ؤ ظْ Fin (N + 1)`. Spin-`S` analogue of ╬│-4 step 216; the per-site sum-of-squares term is the configuration-dependent z-axis squared contribution that doesn't collapse for spin-`S`. Spin-`1/2` is the special case where the term reduces to constant `\|╬ؤ\|/4` (╬│-4 step 218) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1265) |
| `basisVec_totalSpinHalfOp12_sq_expectation_general` | **Spin-`1/2` transverse Casimir `(┼£_tot^(1))┬▓ + (┼£_tot^(2))┬▓` on arbitrary `basisVec ╧â` is constant `\|╬ؤ\|/2`**: independent of `╧â`. Direct corollary of ╬│-4 step 216 + ╬│-4 step 204 (z-axis cancels). Expresses the per-state transverse contribution as a pure combinatorial quantity (╬│-4 step 219) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1266) |
| `basisVecS_totalSpinSOp12_sq_expectation_general` | **Spin-`S` transverse Casimir `(┼£_tot^(1))┬▓ + (┼£_tot^(2))┬▓` on arbitrary `basisVecS ╧â` = `\|V\|┬╖N(N+2)/4 ظêْ ╬ث_x (N/2 ظêْ ╧âx.val)┬▓`**. Direct corollary of ╬│-4 step 218 + ╬│-4 step 205 (`magEigenvalueS┬▓` cancels). Reduces to `\|V\|┬╖N/2` for N├رel on balanced bipartite graphs (matches ╬│-4 step 156) and to `\|╬ؤ\|/2` for spin-`1/2` (╬│-4 step 220) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1267) |
| `neelConfigOfS_z_eigenvalue_sq_sum` | **N├رel per-site z-eigenvalue squared sum**: `╬ث_x (N/2 ظêْ (neelConfigOfS A N x).val)┬▓ = \|V\|┬╖(N/2)┬▓`. Both extremes `╧âx ظêê {0, N}` give `(N/2)┬▓`. Bridges ╬│-4 step 218 (general formula) to existing closed-form N├رel Casimir (╬│-4 step 221) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1268) |
| `allAlignedConfigS_z_eigenvalue_sq_sum` | **AllAligned per-site z-eigenvalue squared sum**: `╬ث_x (N/2 ظêْ (allAlignedConfigS V N c x).val)┬▓ = \|V\|┬╖(N/2 ظêْ c.val)┬▓`. | `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS` | **N├رel `┼£_tot^(3)` eigenvalue**: `magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS A N) = (\|A\| ظêْ \|┬شA\|)┬╖N/2`. Direct from `magSumS_neelConfigOfS` (= `\|┬شA\|┬╖N`) and the filter-card decomposition `\|V\| = \|A\| + \|┬شA\|`. Named lemma extracted from inlined computation in `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_expectation_card_Lambda` (╬│-4 step 224) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeelBasisVecS.lean` (PR #1271) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_expectation_via_general` | **Spin-`S` N├رel Casimir re-derived via the general formula**: `<╬خ_N├رel \| (┼£_tot)┬▓ \| ╬خ_N├رel> = ((\|A\|ظêْ\|┬شA\|)┬╖N/2)┬▓ + \|V\|┬╖N/2` derived from ╬│-4 step 218 + ╬│-4 step 221 + ╬│-4 step 224 + `ring`. Unification check against the existing `_card_Lambda` form (╬│-4 step 127). Validates that the general formula correctly specializes to the N├رel-specific result (╬│-4 step 225) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeelBasisVecS.lean` (PR #1272) |
| `allAlignedStateS_zero/_last_totalSpinSSquared_via_general` | **Spin-`S` allAligned-up/down Casimir re-derived via general formula**: `<╬خ_ظèج \| (┼£_tot)┬▓ \| ╬خ_ظèج> = m_max┬╖(m_max+1)` (and same for `╬خ_ظèح`) where `m_max = \|V\|┬╖N/2`. | `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfSquared_expectation_via_general` | **Spin-`1/2` N├رel Casimir re-derived via general formula**: `<╬خ_N├رel \| (┼£_tot)┬▓ \| ╬خ_N├رel> = ((\|A\|ظêْ\|┬شA\|)/2)┬▓ + \|╬ؤ\|/2`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ╬│-4 step 225 (╬│-4 step 227) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1274) |
| `basisVec_const_totalSpinHalfSquared_expectation_via_general` | **Spin-`1/2` const-state Casimir re-derived via general formula**: `<basisVec(const s) \| (┼£_tot)┬▓ \| basisVec(const s)> = \|╬ؤ\|┬╖(\|╬ؤ\|+2)/4` for any `s : Fin 2`. Composition of ╬│-4 step 216 + `magnetization (const s) = ┬▒\|╬ؤ\|` + `(spinSign s)┬▓ = 1` + `ring`. Recovers existing ╬│-4 step 201 (╬│-4 step 228) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1275) |
| `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS_complement` | **Complement N├رel `┼£_tot^(3)` eigenvalue**: `magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS (┬شA) N) = (\|┬شA\|ظêْ\|A\|)┬╖N/2`. | `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS_complement_simplified` | **Cleaner form** of ╬│-4 step 229 with double negation `!!A` simplified to `A` via `Finset.filter_congr` + `Bool.not_not`: `magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS (┬شA) N) = (\|┬شA\|ظêْ\|A\|)┬╖N/2` (╬│-4 step 230) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1277) |
| `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS_complement_eq_neg` | **Negation relation**: `magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS (┬شA) N) = ظêْmagEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS A N)`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 224 + ╬│-4 step 230 + `ring`. The complement N├رel sits at the opposite `┼£_tot^(3)` eigenvalue (╬│-4 step 231) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1278) |
| `magnetization_neelConfigOf_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`1/2` negation relation**: `magnetization ╬ؤ (neelConfigOf (┬شA)) = ظêْmagnetization ╬ؤ (neelConfigOf A)`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ╬│-4 step 231; direct from existing `magnetization_neelConfigOf{,_complement}` + `ring` (╬│-4 step 232) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1279) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfSquared_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` Casimir is sublattice-swap invariant on N├رel**: `<╬خ_N├رel(┬شA)\|(┼£_tot)┬▓\|╬خ_N├رel(┬شA)> = <╬خ_N├رel(A)\|(┼£_tot)┬▓\|╬خ_N├رel(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 227 (Casimir = `(M/2)┬▓+\|╬ؤ\|/2`) + ╬│-4 step 232 (`M(┬شA) = ظêْM(A)`): `(M)┬▓` is sign-flip invariant (╬│-4 step 233) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1280) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` Casimir is sublattice-swap invariant on N├رel**: `<╬خ_N├رel(┬شA)\|(┼£_tot)┬▓\|╬خ_N├رel(┬شA)> = <╬خ_N├رel(A)\|(┼£_tot)┬▓\|╬خ_N├رel(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 218 + 221 + 231 + `ring`: `magEigenvalueS┬▓` is sign-flip invariant; `╬ث_x m_x┬▓` evaluates to `\|V\|┬╖(N/2)┬▓` for both A and ┬شA. | `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp3_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` `(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓` is sublattice-swap invariant on N├رel**: `<╬خ_N├رel(┬شA)\|(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬خ_N├رel(┬شA)> = <╬خ_N├رel(A)\|(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬خ_N├رel(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 205 + 231 + `ring`: squared eigenvalue is sign-flip invariant (╬│-4 step 235) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1282) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp3_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` `(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓` is sublattice-swap invariant on N├رel**: `<╬خ_N├رel(┬شA)\|(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬خ_N├رel(┬شA)> = <╬خ_N├رel(A)\|(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬خ_N├رel(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 204 + 232 + `ring`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ╬│-4 step 235 (╬│-4 step 236) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1283) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp12_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` transverse Casimir `(┼£^1)┬▓+(┼£^2)┬▓` is sublattice-swap invariant on N├رel**: equal expectations on `╬خ_N├رel(A)` and `╬خ_N├رel(┬شA)`. Both reduce to `\|V\|┬╖N/2` via ╬│-4 step 220 + 221 (╬│-4 step 237) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1284) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp12_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` transverse Casimir invariance on N├رel**: both expectations equal `\|╬ؤ\|/2`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 219 (╧â-independent constant). | `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp3_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`S` linear `┼£_tot^(3)` negates under sublattice swap on N├رel**: `<╬خ_N├رel(┬شA)\|┼£_tot^(3)\|╬خ_N├رel(┬شA)> = ظêْ<╬خ_N├رel(A)\|┼£_tot^(3)\|╬خ_N├رel(A)>`. | `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp3_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`1/2` linear `┼£_tot^(3)` negates under sublattice swap on N├رel**. Direct from ╬│-4 step 207 + 232 + `ring`. | `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp1/2_expectation` | **Spin-`S` N├رel transverse linear expectations vanish**: `<╬خ_N├رel\|┼£_tot^(1,2)\|╬خ_N├رel> = 0`. Together with ╬│-4 step 91 (`<┼£_tot^(3)> = magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS`), gives the complete per-axis profile `(0, 0, (\|A\|ظêْ\|┬شA\|)┬╖N/2)` | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1288) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp1/2_expectation` | **Spin-`1/2` N├رel transverse linear expectations vanish**: same as ╬│-4 step 241 for spin-`1/2`, derived from ╬│-4 step 215 (╬│-4 step 242) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1289) |

The single-cluster Problem 2.5.a modules below are imported from the
build root again after the 2026-05-30 orphan-module sweep had removed
the earlier implementation.  The restored concrete tip is
`Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean`, and the
minimum-eigenvalue bridge in `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianMin.lean`
keeps the abstract Hamiltonian, energy hygiene, concrete
dimer/trimer/quartet/pentamer eigenvalue formulas, and variational
upper/lower-bound consumers live in the default Lean build
(PR #4032; PR #4033; PR #4034; PR #4035; PR #4036).

| `singleClusterHamiltonianS` | **Single-cluster (star-graph) Heisenberg Hamiltonian** (Tasaki Problem 2.5.a, p. 38): `H = ╬ث_{j=1}^z ┼£_0 ┬╖ ┼£_j` on `Fin (z + 1)` with central vertex `0` and `z` leaves. Ground-state energy `ظêْS(1 + zS)` (╬│-5 step 243) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1290) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_isHermitian` | **Hermiticity** of the single-cluster Heisenberg Hamiltonian. Sum of Hermitian `spinSDot 0 j N` over `j ظêê univ.erase 0` (╬│-5 step 244) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1291) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_zero_z` | **Edge case `z = 0`**: `singleClusterHamiltonianS 0 N = 0` since `univ.erase 0 = ظêà` in `Fin 1`. Tasaki's formula `ظêْS(1 + zS)` is intended for `z ظëح 1` (╬│-5 step 245) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1292) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allUp_expectation` | **All-up expectation**: `<╬خ_ظèج\|H\|╬خ_ظèج> = z┬╖(N/2)┬▓` for the single-cluster Hamiltonian. Far above Tasaki's GS energy `ظêْS(1+zS)` since all-up is in the maximum-`s_tot` Casimir sector, not the minimum (╬│-5 step 246) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1293) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allAligned_expectation` | **AllAligned-c expectation**: `<╬خ_aligned(c)\|H\|╬خ_aligned(c)> = z┬╖(N/2 ظêْ c.val)┬▓`. Generalises ╬│-5 step 246 (the `c = 0` case). For `c = Fin.last N` (all-down) gives same `z┬╖(N/2)┬▓` (╬│-5 step 247) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1294) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allDown_expectation` | **All-down expectation**: `<╬خ_ظèح\|H\|╬خ_ظèح> = z┬╖(N/2)┬▓`. Direct specialisation of ╬│-5 step 247 at `c = Fin.last N` (╬│-5 step 248) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1295) |
| `leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Leaf-spin operators on `Fin (z+1)`**: `┼£_R^(╬▒) = ╬ث_{j ظëب 0} onSiteS j ┼£^(╬▒)` for axes `╬▒ = 1, 2, 3`. Sum over leaves of single-site `┼£^(╬▒)` (excludes central vertex `0`). Building blocks for the upcoming Casimir decomposition `H = (┼£_tot┬▓ ظêْ ┼£_0┬▓ ظêْ ┼£_R┬▓)/2` (╬│-5 step 249) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1296) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eq_dot_leaves` | **`┼£_0 ┬╖ ┼£_R` decomposition**: `H = ╬ث_╬▒ onSiteS 0 (┼£^(╬▒)) ┬╖ leafSpinSOp_╬▒`. Direct distribution of left multiplication over the sum `╬ث_j (A * B_j) = A * (╬ث_j B_j)` via `Finset.mul_sum` (╬│-5 step 250) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1297) |
| `totalSpinSOp1/2/3_eq_onSite_zero_add_leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Total = central + leaves** (per axis): `totalSpinSOp_╬▒ (Fin (z+1)) N = onSiteS 0 (┼£^(╬▒)) + leafSpinSOp_╬▒ z N`. Direct from `Finset.sum_erase_add` + `add_comm` (╬│-5 step 251) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1298) |

| `sublatticeSpinDot` / `sublatticeSpinDot_complement_isHermitian` | cross-sublattice spin dot product: `┼£_A ┬╖ ┼£_B := ╬ث_╬▒ ┼£_A^(╬▒) ┼£_B^(╬▒)`. `┼£_A ┬╖ ┼£_┬شA` is Hermitian (each summand is the product of two commuting Hermitian operators). Bilinear expansion `sublatticeSpinDot_eq_sum_sum`: `┼£_A ┬╖ ┼£_B = ╬ث_{x : A x} ╬ث_{y : B y} ┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y` connects the operator-level Casimir form to the bond-level Heisenberg expression in the toy Hamiltonian (Tasaki ┬د2.5 (2.5.10)) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinDot.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_eq_sum_dot` / `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_basisVec_const` / `_all_up` / `_all_down` / `_of_const_on` | `(┼£_A)┬▓ = ╬ث_{x ظêê A} ╬ث_{y ظêê A} ┼£_x ┬╖ ┼£_y` (specialisation `B = A` of the bilinear expansion), and the **maximum-spin Casimir eigenvalue on the all-aligned state**: `(┼£_A)┬▓ ┬╖ \|s s ظخ sظار = (\|A\|┬╖(\|A\|+2)/4) ┬╖ \|s s ظخ sظار` for any `s : Fin 2`. Generalised form `_of_const_on`: any `\|╧âظار` with `╧â` **constant on `A`** is an eigenvector with eigenvalue `\|A\|┬╖(\|A\|+2)/4` (relevant for N├رel-style states which are constant on each sublattice but not globally) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_eq_sublatticeSpinDot_sum` | the MLM toy Hamiltonian decomposes as an oriented cross-sublattice spin dot product: `─ج_toy = ┼£_A ┬╖ ┼£_┬شA + ┼£_┬شA ┬╖ ┼£_A`. Bridges the bipartite-bond sum (Tasaki ┬د2.5 (2.5.10)) to the operator-level Casimir form (Tasaki ┬د2.5 (2.5.11)) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinDot_complement_comm` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_eq_two_sublatticeSpinDot` | cross-sublattice symmetry: `┼£_A ┬╖ ┼£_┬شA = ┼£_┬شA ┬╖ ┼£_A` (each axis pair commutes by `sublatticeSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}_cross_commute`), giving the closed form `─ج_toy = 2 ظت ┼£_A ┬╖ ┼£_┬شA` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_eq_sublattice_casimir` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_eq_casimir_diff` | **Casimir identity** (Tasaki ┬د2.5 (2.5.11)): `(┼£_tot)┬▓ = (┼£_A)┬▓ + 2 ظت (┼£_A ┬╖ ┼£_┬شA) + (┼£_┬شA)┬▓` (per-axis `(a + b)┬▓ = a┬▓ + 2ab + b┬▓` via cross-commute), and the closed form (without `1/|╬ؤ|`) `─ج_toy = (┼£_tot)┬▓ ظêْ (┼£_A)┬▓ ظêْ (┼£_┬شA)┬▓` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared` | the toy Hamiltonian commutes with `(┼£_tot)┬▓` (specialisation of `heisenbergHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared`). The standard fact used to project the toy ground state onto a fixed `(┼£_tot)┬▓` eigenspace, underpinning the `S_tot = 0` selection of the toy ground state | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_commute_sublatticeSpinHalfSquared` / `_complement` | the toy Hamiltonian commutes with `(┼£_A)┬▓` and with `(┼£_┬شA)┬▓`. Direct consequences of the closed form `─ج_toy = (┼£_tot)┬▓ ظêْ (┼£_A)┬▓ ظêْ (┼£_┬شA)┬▓` and the three pairwise Casimir commutativities (PRs ╬▒-6o, ╬▒-6s, self-commute trivially). Together with ╬▒-6p, gives all four Casimir-style commutators of `─ج_toy`, the prerequisite for the joint eigenbasis analysis on which the `S_tot = 0` selection rests | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` / `_simplified` | explicit eigenvalue of `─ج_toy` on the all-aligned state: the Casimir-difference form `\|╬ؤ\|┬╖(\|╬ؤ\|+2)/4 ظêْ \|A\|┬╖(\|A\|+2)/4 ظêْ \|┬شA\|┬╖(\|┬شA\|+2)/4` algebraically simplifies via `\|╬ؤ\| = \|A\| + \|┬شA\|` to the **product form** `\|A\|┬╖\|┬شA\|/2`. The eigenvalue is non-negative for any bipartite lattice and strictly positive when both sublattices are non-empty | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_neelStateOf` / `_complement_mulVec_neelStateOf` | sublattice Casimir eigenvalues on the **N├رel state** `╬خ_N├رel(A) := basisVec (neelConfigOf A)`: `(┼£_A)┬▓ ┬╖ \|╬خ_N├رel(A)ظار = (\|A\|┬╖(\|A\|+2)/4) ┬╖ \|╬خ_N├رel(A)ظار` and `(┼£_┬شA)┬▓ ┬╖ \|╬خ_N├رel(A)ظار = (\|┬شA\|┬╖(\|┬شA\|+2)/4) ┬╖ \|╬خ_N├رel(A)ظار`. Direct corollaries of `_of_const_on` since the N├رel configuration is constant on each sublattice (`╧â x = 0` on `A`, `╧â x = 1` on `┬شA`); the N├رel state is simultaneously a `(┼£_A)┬▓` and `(┼£_┬شA)┬▓` eigenvector at maximum-spin eigenvalues | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelCore.lean` |
| `mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_commute_of_mem` | generic preservation: any operator `A` with `Commute A (┼£tot^(3))` maps each magnetisation sector `H_M` to itself ظ¤ operator-level form of Tasaki ┬د2.2 (2.2.10), p. 22 block-diagonalisation | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | Casimir specialisation: `┼£tot┬▓` preserves every `H_M` (since `[┼£tot┬▓, ┼£tot^(3)] = 0`) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `J : ╬ؤ ظْ ╬ؤ ظْ ظé` and `M : ظé`, `v ظêê H_M` implies `H ┬╖ v ظêê H_M` ظ¤ the operator-level statement that any Heisenberg Hamiltonian block-diagonalises against Tasaki ┬د2.2 (2.2.10), p. 22 magnetisation-sector decomposition (consequence of SU(2) invariance `[H, ┼£tot^(3)] = 0`) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpMinus_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `M : ظé`, `v ظêê H_M` implies `┼£tot^- ┬╖ v ظêê H_{M-1}` ظ¤ the standard SU(2) lowering ladder action via the Cartan relation `[┼£tot^(3), ┼£tot^-] = -┼£tot^-` | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpPlus_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `M : ظé`, `v ظêê H_M` implies `┼£tot^+ ┬╖ v ظêê H_{M+1}` ظ¤ the SU(2) raising ladder action via `[┼£tot^(3), ┼£tot^+] = +┼£tot^+` | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_two_site` | for `╬ؤ = Fin 2` and any `╬╕`, the global rotation factors as `onSite 0 (├ؤ^(╬▒)_╬╕) * onSite 1 (├ؤ^(╬▒)_╬╕)` (general-╬╕ extension of Problem 2.2.b) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `onSite_exp_eq_exp_onSite` | `onSite x (exp A) = exp (onSite x A)` ظ¤ bridge between single-site and many-body matrix exponential. Local Frobenius normed structure + `LinearMap.continuous_of_finiteDimensional` + `NormedSpace.map_exp` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |

### One-dimensional open-chain quantum Ising

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬د3.3 eq. (3.3.1), p. 55 (transverse-field Ising on an open
chain). Our formalization uses the Pauli convention `╧â = 2┬╖S` and an
explicit bond coupling `J`.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h` | `H = -J ╬ث ╧â^z_i ╧â^z_{i+1} - h ╬ث ╧â^x_i` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian_isHermitian` | `H` is Hermitian for real `J`, `h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `spinZDot x y` | the Ising bond operator `╧â^z_x ┬╖ ╧â^z_y` (generic in `╬ؤ`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `spinZDot_isHermitian` | each bond operator is Hermitian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianGeneric (J : ╬ؤ ظْ ╬ؤ ظْ ظé) (h : ظé)` | **graph-friendly** Ising Hamiltonian `╬ث J(x,y) ╧â^z_x ╧â^z_y ظêْ h ╬ث ╧â^x_x` for any finite `╬ؤ` and pairwise coupling `J`; specialises to chain / graph / lattice via the choice of `J` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianGeneric_isHermitian` | Hermitian for entry-wise real `J` and real `h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianOnGraph G J h` | graph wrapper: `isingHamiltonianGeneric (couplingOf G J) h`; double-sum convention matches Heisenberg-on-graph | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianOnGraph_isHermitian` | Hermitian for real `J, h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingGibbsStateOnGraph G ╬▓ J h` | Gibbs state of the graph-built Ising Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingGibbsStateOnGraph_isHermitian` / `isingGibbsStateOnGraph_commute_hamiltonian` | Hermiticity (real `J, h`) and commute with the Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.sum_pathGraph_forward` / `sum_pathGraph_backward` / `sum_pathGraph_adj` | sum-decomposition helpers for `pathGraph (N+1)` adjacency: `╬ث_{x,y}` over ordered adjacent pairs = `╬ث_{i:Fin N} (f i.cs i.s + f i.s i.cs)` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `pathGraphParityColoring` / `pathGraph_isBipartite` | parity-based 2-colouring of `pathGraph (N + 1)` (`i ظخ i.val % 2`) and the corresponding `IsBipartite` proof. Underpins the N├رel state (`Quantum/NeelState.lean`) and the Marshall-Lieb-Mattis theorem (Tasaki ┬د2.5) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `cycleGraphEvenParityColoring` / `cycleGraph_even_isBipartite` | parity-based 2-colouring of the even cycle `cycleGraph (2 * K + 2)` and the corresponding `IsBipartite` proof. Wrap-around case `(2K+1) + 1 ظëة 0` still flips parity because the cycle length is even (odd cycles are not bipartite) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian_eq_isingHamiltonianGeneric` | **generic-N bridge**: `quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h = isingHamiltonianGeneric (couplingOf (pathGraph (N+1)) (-J/2)) h`. The proof itself is the robust regression test | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |

### Gibbs state (Tasaki ┬د3.3)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬د3.3.

All theorems in this module are fully proved with **zero `sorry`**.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `gibbsExp ╬▓ H` | `exp(-╬▓H) := Matrix.exp (-╬▓ ظت H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_isHermitian` | `exp(-╬▓H)` is Hermitian (when `H` is Hermitian) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_zero` | `exp(-0┬╖H) = 1` (Tasaki ┬د3.3, pp. 75ظô78) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |

| `gibbsExp_add_of_commute_hamiltonians` | `exp(-╬▓(Hظé+Hظéé)) = exp(-╬▓Hظé) ┬╖ exp(-╬▓Hظéé)` for commuting `Hظé, Hظéé` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |

| `gibbsState_inv` | `(╧_╬▓)ظ╗┬╣ = Z(╬▓) ┬╖ e^{╬▓H}` when `Z(╬▓) ظëب 0` (general matrix inverse, generalises `gibbsState_zero_inv`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬د3.5, p. 89.

| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `H_open(N=1) ┬╖ |ظّظّظار = -(J/2) ┬╖ |ظّظّظار` ظ¤ `|ظّظّظار` lies in the `S = 1` triplet sector and is an exact eigenvector with eigenvalue `-J/2` (this is the ferromagnetic ground state for `J < 0`) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_singlet` | `H_open(N=1) ┬╖ (|ظّظôظار - |ظôظّظار) = (3J/2) ┬╖ (|ظّظôظار - |ظôظّظار)` ظ¤ singlet eigenvalue, the antiferromagnetic ground state for `J > 0` (Tasaki ┬د2.5 concrete instance) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_down` | `H_open(N=1) ┬╖ |ظôظôظار = -(J/2) ┬╖ |ظôظôظار` ظ¤ all-down state has the same eigenvalue as all-up (both are `S = 1` triplet states) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_triplet_zero` | `H_open(N=1) ┬╖ (|ظّظôظار + |ظôظّظار) = -(J/2) ┬╖ (|ظّظôظار + |ظôظّظار)` ظ¤ triplet `m = 0` state, completing the 3-fold degenerate triplet representation `S = 1` with eigenvalue `-J/2` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_three_site_eq` | for `N = 2` (the 3-site open chain on `Fin 3`, 2 bonds), `H_open = -2J ┬╖ (spinHalfDot 0 1 + spinHalfDot 1 2)` ظ¤ explicit two-bond reduction | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_three_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `H_open(N=2) ┬╖ |ظّظّظّظار = -J ┬╖ |ظّظّظّظار` ظ¤ confirming the linear scaling `E(|ظّ..ظّظار) = -N┬╖J/2` (here `N = 2` bonds, `J = 1` per bond) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_sum_eq` | for any `N : ظـ`, `╬ث_{x,y ظêê Fin (N+1)} openChainCoupling N J x y = -(2N ┬╖ J : ظé)` (the bond-counting lemma: each of the `N` unordered nearest-neighbour bonds is counted in both orientations) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` | for any `N : ظـ`, `J : ظإ`, and constant `s : Fin 2`, `H_open ┬╖ |s..sظار = -(N┬╖J/2 : ظé) ┬╖ |s..sظار` ظ¤ both `s = 0` (all-up) and `s = 1` (all-down) share the same eigenvalue by SU(2) symmetry | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `s = 0` specialisation of the above (Tasaki ┬د2.4 (2.4.5)/(2.4.1) ferromagnetic ground-state energy `E_GS = -|B|┬╖S┬▓` for `S = 1/2`, `|B| = N` bonds) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_down` | `s = 1` specialisation: same eigenvalue `-(N┬╖J/2)` for the all-down state by SU(2) symmetry | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_pow_basisVec_all_up` | for any `N : ظـ`, `J : ظإ`, `k : ظـ`, `H_open ┬╖ ((┼£tot^-)^k ┬╖ |ظّ..ظّظار) = -(N┬╖J/2 : ظé) ┬╖ ((┼£tot^-)^k ┬╖ |ظّ..ظّظار)` ظ¤ the unnormalised Tasaki ┬د2.4 (2.4.9) ferromagnetic ground states `|╬خ_Mظار` made explicit on the chain (combines PRs #82 + #98) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_pow_basisVec_all_down` | dual ladder from `|ظô..ظôظار`, same eigenvalue `-(N┬╖J/2)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | `H_open` preserves every magnetisation subspace `H_M` (chain specialisation of PR #91) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | `H_periodic` preserves every magnetisation subspace `H_M` (chain specialisation of PR #91) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |

### Perron-Frobenius theorem (`Math/PerronFrobenius.lean`, `Math/PerronFrobeniusPrimitive.lean`, `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean`, `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean`)

Perron-Frobenius theorem for nonneg irreducible/primitive matrices (Issue #405, closed).
The sorry in `exists_pos_eigenvec_max` is eliminated via the Collatz-Wielandt port (PRs AظôC).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `Matrix.IsPrimitive.of_irreducible_pos_diagonal` | irreducible nonneg + positive diagonal ظْ primitive (Seneta ┬د1.1, Prop. 1.3, p. 17) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusPrimitive.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.collatzWielandtFn` | CW function `min_{i\|x_i>0} (Ax)_i/x_i` (Seneta ┬د1.2, p. 27) | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.le_any_ratio` | `CW(x) ظëج (Ax)_i/x_i` for `x_i > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.le_mulVec` | fundamental inequality `CW(x)┬╖x ظëج Ax` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.smul_eq` | scale invariance `CW(cx) = CW(x)` for `c > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.upperSemicontinuousOn` | CW is upper-semicontinuous on stdSimplex | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.exists_maximizer` | CW attains its max on stdSimplex (EVT for USC, Seneta ┬د1.2, p. 28) | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.eq_eigenvalue` | `CW(v) = r` when `Av = r┬╖v`, `v > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.lt_of_all_ratios_gt` | all ratios `> c` ظا╣ `CW(x) > c` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.pos_of_nonneg_eigenvec` | irreducible nonneg + `Av = ╬╝v`, `v ظëح 0`, `v ظëب 0` ظا╣ `v > 0` (standard propagation argument) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.exists_positive_eigenvector_of_primitive` | primitive nonneg ظا╣ ظêâ `r > 0`, `v > 0` with `Av = rv` (Seneta ┬د1.2, pp. 27ظô28) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.exists_positive_eigenvector_of_irreducible` | irreducible nonneg ظا╣ ظêâ `r > 0`, `v > 0` with `Av = rv` (Seneta ┬د1.2, pp. 27ظô28) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `exists_pos_eigenvec_max` | (**sorry-free**) irreducible nonneg Hermitian ظا╣ max eigenvalue has strictly positive eigenvector | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `pos_eigenvec_unique` | strictly positive eigenvector unique up to positive scalar | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `PerronFrobenius.eigenvec_proportional_of_pos_eigenvec` | **geometric simplicity of the Perron eigenvalue**: irreducible nonneg with a strictly positive eigenvector `v` at `╬╝` ظا╣ every real eigenvector at `╬╝` is `s ظت v` for some scalar `s` (the `╬╝`-eigenspace is 1-dimensional). Perturbation trick: `v + t ظت u > 0` for small `t > 0` is an eigenvector at `╬╝`, hence `ظêإ v` by `pos_eigenvec_unique`, forcing `u ظêإ v`. Used to show a Perron ground state is a joint eigenvector of any commuting operator (e.g. the total Casimir) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusSimple.lean` |

References: E. Seneta, *Non-negative Matrices and Markov Chains* (3rd ed.), Springer 2006, ┬د1.2 (pp. 27ظô28);
or4nge19/MCMC: `MCMC/PF/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/PerronFrobenius/`.

### Spin-`S` MarshallظôLiebظôMattis on the magnetization sector (Tasaki ┬د2.5 Theorem 2.2 generic S, sector form)

> **Route correction (2026-05-26).** The earlier "saturated-ladder-iterate"
> route toward `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` was found **unsound**: it reduced the
> theorem to a leaf hypothesis (lowerable attach-sum dominance) that is **false**
> ظ¤ the ferromagnetic ladder iterate `(┼£ظ╗_tot)^M|all-upظار` is *not* the
> Marshall-positive antiferromagnetic ground state (concrete counterexample:
> spin-1/2, `V={a,b}`, `A={a}`, `B={b}`, `M=0ظْ1`). The 40 conditional-wrapper
> modules of that route were removed; their detailed rows below were deleted with
> them. The **sound** route keeps the per-sector PerronظôFrobenius ground state
> (`tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_sector_existence`, sorry-free) and chains sectors by
> SU(2) invariance `[─ج, ┼£ظ╗_tot] = 0`; some rows below still reference helper
> lemmas from the removed route pending re-documentation. See
> `.self-local/docs/tasaki-2-5-pf-route-design.md` and Issue #3542.

Generic-spin (`N = 2S`) version of Tasaki ┬د2.5 Theorem 2.2 applied to the
**magnetization-`M` sector** of the un-dressed antiferromagnetic
Heisenberg Hamiltonian on a bipartite graph. The sector subtype
`magConfigS V N M := { ╧â : V ظْ Fin (N + 1) // magSumS ╧â = M }` is the
natural index type since the dressed Heisenberg matrix is irreducible
on each sector. The core sector-matrix API lives in
`Quantum/SpinS/DressedMatrixOnMagSector.lean`; the trailing
eigenvalue-uniqueness + complex-form MLM Theorem 2.2 statements
were moved to the companion
`Quantum/SpinS/DressedMatrixOnMagSectorEigenvalueUnique.lean` in
refactor #30 (PR #2829). The two bundled sector-level MLM Theorem 2.2
ground-state results (`marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergSector_groundState`,
`marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergSector_complexGroundState_full`) were
subsequently relocated to
`Quantum/SpinS/MarshallLiebMattisSectorBundled.lean` (#4569, re-proved via the
canonical `*Structural*` twins). Tracked in Issue #412.

| Lean name | Statement |
|---|---|
| `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ` | **Sound PF adjacent-sector ladder link (lowering)**: if a magnetization-`M` sector vector `magSectorEmbedding ╬خ` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at energy `╬╝` and a total-Casimir eigenvector at `╬│` away from the sector's lowering-kernel value `(card┬╖N/2ظêْM)(card┬╖N/2ظêْMظêْ1)`, then `┼£ظ╗_tot ┬╖ magSectorEmbedding ╬خ` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at the **same** `╬╝` (via `[─ج, ┼£ظ╗_tot]=0`), is non-zero (total-Casimir non-vanishing criterion), and lies in the next magnetization sector. This is the energy-preserving step that chains the per-sector PerronظôFrobenius ground states (`tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_sector_existence`) across the admissible range; it uses **no** Marshall positivity of the lowered vector (the false hypothesis of the removed ferromagnetic-ladder route). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬د2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PFLadderLink.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_pred` / `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_pred_of_mem_predictedGS` | **Sound PF adjacent-sector ladder link (raising)** and its predicted-GS specialisation: the raising companions of `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ(_of_mem_predictedGS)`. `┼£ظ║_tot` maps a sector-`K` Heisenberg eigenvector (total-Casimir away from the raising-kernel value `(card┬╖N/2ظêْK)(card┬╖N/2ظêْK+1)`) to a non-zero same-energy eigenvector in the previous sector; predicted-GS membership in sector `M+1` strictly above the left endpoint discharges the Casimir hypotheses via `tasaki23_predictedCasimirValue_ne_raising_kernel_value_of_mem_of_left_lt`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬د2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PFLadderLink.lean`) |
| `sublatticeMagSubspaceS` / `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS` (with `mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS_iff`, `basisVecS_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS`, `sublatticeMagSubspaceS_disjoint`) | **Sublattice magnetization grading**: the `┼£_A^(3)`-eigenspaces `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M` and the diagonal eigenvalue `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A ╧â = ظêّ_{xظêêA}(N/2ظêْ╧â_x)`. Submodule API (zero/add/smul/neg/sub/finite-sum membership), disjointness of distinct eigenvalues, and basis-state membership. Sublattice analogue of `magSubspaceS`/`magEigenvalueS`; scaffold (Issue #3658, PR 5) for the sublattice highest-weight argument bounding `(┼£_A)┬▓`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬د2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagnetization.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_groundState_sublattice_casimir_re_le` | **Sublattice Casimir bounds for the toy ground state**: the per-sector Marshall-positive toy GS is a joint Casimir eigenvector (#3657) whose `(┼£_A)┬▓`/`(┼£_┬شA)┬▓` eigenvalues `╬│_A, ╬│_B` obey `╬│_A.re ظëج s_A(s_A+1)`, `╬│_B.re ظëج s_B(s_B+1)` (apply the spectral max bound #3672 to the non-zero Marshall-dressed embedding). Discharges two hypotheses of the toy total-Casimir pin #3676 (Issue #3674, PR 3). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬د2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23StructuralPFJointCasimir.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_extremal_sector_magnetization_re_eq_predicted` | **Extremal sector realizes the predicted total spin**: for the left-endpoint sector `M = min(|A|,|┬شA|)┬╖N`, the magnetization eigenvalue `|V|┬╖N/2 ظêْ M` has real part `= tasaki23PredictedTotalSpin A N = |s_A ظêْ s_B|`. Supplies the extremal-sector hypothesis `hM` of the toy total-Casimir pin #3676 (Issue #3674, PR 4). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬د2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ExtremalSector.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_groundState_casimir_eq_predicted_of_energy_le` | **Toy ground state is the predicted-Casimir witness (modulo the energy bound)**: in the extremal sector `M = min(|A|,|┬شA|)┬╖N`, the Marshall-positive toy GS with toy energy `╬╝ ظëج predicted ظêْ s_A(s_A+1) ظêْ s_B(s_B+1)` is a `(┼£_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at exactly `tasaki23PredictedCasimirValue A N`. Capstone assembling the joint eigenvector (#3657), sublattice bounds (#3677), toy energy formula (#3673), extremal sector (#3678), the two-sided pin (#3676), and Hermitian eigenvalue realness ظ¤ exactly the witness form required by #3656. The single remaining obligation is the reference energy bound `h╬╝` (Issue #3674, PR 5). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬د2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyWitness.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_sector_groundEnergy_le_of_witness` | **Toy ground energy is minimal in its sector**: the Marshall-positive toy ground state (energy `╬╝`) has `╬╝ ظëج E` for every sector eigenvector `╧ ظëب 0` of the toy Heisenberg sector matrix at real energy `E`. Specialises `heisenbergHamiltonianSReMatrixOnMagSector_eigenvalue_ge_of_marshallPositive` to `J = bipartiteCoupling A`; reduces the reference energy bound `h╬╝` (and hence the whole route, via #3679) to exhibiting one sector eigenvector at the predicted ground energy `predicted ظêْ s_A(s_A+1) ظêْ s_B(s_B+1)` (Issue #3674, PR 6). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬د2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyGroundEnergyBound.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_total_casimir_re_eq_predicted_of_bounds` (with `tasaki23PredictedTotalSpin_nonneg`) | **Toy total-Casimir pin (extremal sector)**: in the extremal magnetization sector `M.re = |s_Aظêْs_B|`, a non-zero `(┼£_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│_tot` with sublattice Casimir max bounds `╬│_A ظëج s_A(s_A+1)`, `╬│_B ظëج s_B(s_B+1)` and reference toy energy bound `(╬│_totظêْ╬│_Aظêْ╬│_B).re ظëج predicted ظêْ s_A(s_A+1) ظêْ s_B(s_B+1)` has `╬│_tot.re = predicted`. A two-sided squeeze (magnetization lower bound #3675 + sublattice upper bounds #3672 + energy formula #3673), isolating the extremal-sector and reference-energy obligations as hypotheses; no ClebschظôGordan (Issue #3674, PR 2). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬د2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyCasimirPin.lean`) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_ge_of_mem_magSubspaceS` (with `exists_highestWeight_eigenvector_ge`) | **Total-Casimir magnetization lower bound** `(┼£_tot)┬▓ ظëح m(m+1)`: a non-zero `(┼£_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` in `magSubspaceS V N M` with `0 ظëج M.re` has `M.re┬╖(M.re+1) ظëج ╬│.re`. The total spin dominates its `z`-projection: raising with `┼£ظ║_tot` only increases the magnetization, so the highest weight `M'` reached has `M' ظëح M`, giving `╬│ = M'(M'+1) ظëح M(M+1)`. Lower-bound companion of `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_le_sMax`; in the extremal sector `m = s_Aظêْs_B` it pins the toy GS total Casimir to predicted, bypassing the ClebschظôGordan triangle inequality (Issue #3674, PR 1). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬د2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirSpectralLowerBound.lean`) |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_mulVec_of_joint_casimir_eigenvector` | **Toy energy on a joint Casimir eigenvector**: a simultaneous eigenvector of `(┼£_tot)┬▓`, `(┼£_A)┬▓`, `(┼£_┬شA)┬▓` at `(╬│_tot, ╬│_A, ╬│_B)` is a `─ج_toy`-eigenvector at `╬│_tot ظêْ ╬│_A ظêْ ╬│_B` (from `─ج_toy = (┼£_tot)┬▓ظêْ(┼£_A)┬▓ظêْ(┼£_┬شA)┬▓`). The energy formula underlying the variational identification of the toy ground state's predicted total Casimir (Issue #3658, PR 4 step 1). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬د2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/ToyHamiltonianJointEnergy.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eigenvalue_re_le_sA` | **Sublattice Casimir spectral max bound** `(┼£_A)┬▓ ظëج s_A(s_A+1)`: every eigenvalue `╬│` of `(┼£_A)┬▓` (with a non-zero eigenvector) has `╬│.re ظëج s_A(s_A+1)`, `s_A = |A|┬╖N/2`. Assembles the sublattice weight-component extraction (#3670), highest-weight existence (#3671), and the highest-weight bound (#3668). Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_le_sMax`; the sublattice analytic ingredient of the Issue #3658 witness construction (Issue #3658, PR 12). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬د2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirSpectralBound.lean`) |
| `sublatticeExists_highestWeight_eigenvector` (with `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS_ne_sMax_add_one`) | **Sublattice highest-weight existence**: from a non-zero `(┼£_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` in `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A (s_Aظêْk)`, repeatedly raising with `┼£_A^+` (which terminates once the sublattice magnetization exceeds `s_A`, since the subspace at `s_A+1` is `ظèح`) produces a non-zero highest-weight `(┼£_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at the same `╬│`. Sublattice analogue of `exists_highestWeight_eigenvector` (Issue #3658, PR 11). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬د2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeHighestWeightExistence.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eigenvec_exists_weight_component` (with `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_sublatticeMagProjFn_eq`, `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS`) | **Sublattice weight-component extraction**: since `(┼£_A)┬▓` commutes with `┼£_A^(3)` it preserves each sublattice magnetization subspace and commutes with the projector; hence a non-zero `(┼£_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` has a non-zero sublattice-weight component `sublatticeMagProjFn A (s_Aظêْk) v` that is again a `(┼£_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` in a single sublattice magnetization subspace. Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvec_exists_weight_component` (Issue #3658, PR 10). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬د2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagWeightComponent.lean`) |
| `sublatticeMagProjFn` / `sum_sublatticeMagProjFn_eq` (with `sublatticeMagSumS`, `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS_eq_sub`, `sublatticeMagProjFn_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS`) | **Sublattice magnetization projection & decomposition**: the pointwise projector `sublatticeMagProjFn A M v` (keeps `v ╧â` where `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A ╧â = M`) lands in `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M`, is linear, and the projectors over the finite spectrum `s_A ظêْ k` (`k=0..|A|┬╖N`) sum to the identity (`sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A ╧â = s_A ظêْ sublatticeMagSumS A ╧â`, `sublatticeMagSumS A ╧â = ظêّ_{xظêêA}╧â_x ظëج |A|┬╖N`). Sublattice analogue of `magProjFn`/`sum_magProjFn_eq` (Issue #3658, PR 9). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬د2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagProjection.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_highestWeight_eigenvalue_re_le` (with `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS_im_zero`) | **Sublattice highest-weight Casimir bound**: a non-zero highest-weight `(┼£_A)┬▓`-eigenvector (`┼£_A^+ w = 0`) at `╬│` in `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M` satisfies `╬│.re ظëج s_A(s_A+1)`, `s_A = |A|┬╖N/2`. Its Casimir value is `M(M+1)` (#3663), `M` is an attained basis eigenvalue (#3667 ظèح-lemma), so `|M| ظëج s_A` (#3660). Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSSquared_highestWeight_eigenvalue_re_le` (Issue #3658, PR 8). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬د2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirHighestWeightBound.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOp3_apply_diag` / `sublatticeSpinSOp3_apply_off_diag` / `sublatticeMagSubspaceS_eq_bot_of_not_in_spectrum` | **Sublattice magnetization spectrum**: `┼£_A^(3)` is diagonal with entry `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A ╧â` on the diagonal and zero off it; hence a value `M` outside the diagonal spectrum gives `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M = ظèح`. Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSOp3_apply_diag/off_diag` and `magSubspaceS_eq_bot_of_not_in_spectrum`; lets the highest-weight argument conclude an attained sublattice magnetization is an actual basis eigenvalue (so `|M| ظëج s_A`) (Issue #3658, PR 7). | `matrix_similar_eigenspace_inter_finrank_eq` | **(g.3) similarity-invariant intersection eigenspace finrank** (Tasaki ┬د2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3835): generalises `matrix_similar_eigenspace_finrank_eq` (#3746 family) to intersections with a second operator's eigenspace, provided the similarity `U` commutes with that operator `Q`. Given `U * Uinv = 1`, `Uinv * U = 1`, `H' = Uinv * H * U`, AND `Q * U = U * Q`, then `finrank ظé (eig H' ╬╝ ظèô ker(Q=c)) = finrank ظé (eig H ╬╝ ظèô ker(Q=c))`. The core technical step for transferring per-parity-block PF bounds from the dressed `─ج'_dressed` to the bare `─ج'`, since the Marshall sign diagonal `╬ء_A` commutes with the diagonal `magParityDiagS P`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬د2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43ظô44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/GaugeIntersectionEigenspaceFinrank.lean`) |

The direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input is superseded by the strict-gap wrappers in the next row; for case (ii), the conditional, strict-gap, and no-full-finrank bridges are recorded in the case-(ii) rows and the strict-gap derivation remains explicit. The remaining case-(ii) mathematical input is now pathwise axis-swapped parity-block submatrix simplicity at the full ground energy.
